programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: In a first for the show, Jackson Galaxy helps a cat that cannot get along in its rescue home to move in with a foster family, hoping they will eventually adopt the fussy feline

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson comes to the aid of room-mates Josh and Grace, who cannot keep their cats from attacking each other. He also helps a mother and daughter struggling with a fearful feral cat

programme.title: Edge of Alaska

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Seven Deadly Sins
programme.desc: Actor Richard E Grant explores the origins of the seven deadly sins, examining the dramatic effect they have. He explores the roots of envy - the resentful longing for other people's possessions or qualities - and also investigates if it exists in the animal kingdom

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete creates a treehouse cinema, with loungers elevated into the trees to give a perfect view of the screen and food and drinks served by way of a pulley system

programme.desc: An investigator intervenes at a private animal shelter for 600 dogs, trying to persuade the owner she should reduce numbers in the pack

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins
programme.desc: Terri helps determine if Caballe the rhino is pregnant, while Robert matchmakes the zoo's Aldabra giant tortoises

programme.title: Lone Star Law

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff heads to Texas to help out rescued animals after Hurricane Harvey. Back in Denver, a woman brings in Bear, who needs surgery after eating a sock

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Rocky, a young pitbull, fights for his life after being bitten by a rattlesnake, while Sassquatch the cat suffers after a fire at his owner's apartment

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: A hurricane is heading towards New Orleans. M2 and Kenani rush to prepare for baby number two and Tia and Tania's rescue mission takes a sudden turn

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy enters the Santé D'or shelter with the intention of helping an over-aggressive shelter cat who goes by the name of Pig Pen

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps the owners of Scout, who is so afraid of his tormentor Caramel that he lives inside a kitchen cabinet

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete creates a treehouse cinema, with loungers elevated into the trees to give a perfect view of the screen and food and drinks served by way of a pulley system

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on people who keep all manner of animals as pets. In the first edition, a so-called 'lion whisperer' in the Czech Republic talks about his big cats. Plus, the story of a Texan cowboy who lets buffalo roam in his house

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Fun facts about animals, including how swarms of blind bats avoid collision, whether mice really like cheese, why slugs are so slimy and what makes manatees' farts so special

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How humpback whales are able to leap so far out of the water, why dogs tilt their heads and how do spiders shed their own skin. Plus, why do animals gather in swarms?

programme.title: Tiger Shark King
programme.desc: Documentary examining the secret lives of tiger sharks, one of the most deadly predators in the oceans

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: As the battle for McCarthy wages on, a team of experts work to locate the only known entrance into the Mother Lode mine

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: A Irish bar in Florida requests a pub-themed aquarium, while a jerky store in Las Vegas wants a butcher-block tank

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: When the Moon(shine) Hits Your Eye
programme.desc: Wayde King and Brett Raymer travel to Tennessee, where the Hatfield and McCoy's dinner theatre wants to replace an old moonshine still with an aquarium

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: In Mexico, the crew navigate a maze of crocodile and mosquito-infested waterways and lagoons in search of a rumoured paradise for fishing tarpon

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil's project is over budget, so his partner moves to push him out of his oldest business, while Jeremy and his boys go on a supply run and return with Jason Lobo

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A mother is sceptical when her children say they have seen a figure in the bathroom and a man covered in blood in the basement

programme.title: Tiger Shark King
programme.desc: Documentary examining the secret lives of tiger sharks, one of the most deadly predators in the oceans

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Marvin is reduced to tears by the sight of a pit bull puppy, an encounter with a snake makes Seth a prisoner in his own home and arachnophobe Jahara refuses to open any windows

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on December 3, 1999. Featuring Melanie C, Boyzone, Alice Deejay, Lolly, Phats & Small, Martine McCutcheon and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Too Near the Sun
programme.desc: The history of the super-bomb, following the race by scientists to imitate the nuclear reactions of the sun and to build the first thermonuclear weapon. Also, exploring how the careers and reputations of some of the scientists involved were seared in the process. First broadcast June 9, 1966

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Mathematicians v Cambridge Quiz Society: Semi-Final
programme.desc: Three mathematicians pit their wits against the Cambridge Quiz Society, to make connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the first semi-final of the quiz show

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon charts the history of American art on a coast-to-coast journey, beginning in the east, where he discusses how the country's rise to power was underpinned by the destruction of Native American cultural heritage. He examines portraits of Puritan settlers in Massachusetts, tells the story of a famous painting of Quaker settler William Penn, reads one of the world's most expensive books in Philadelphia and explore the wilderness landscapes that inspired artist Thomas Cole

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on December 3, 1999. Featuring Melanie C, Boyzone, Alice Deejay, Lolly, Phats & Small, Martine McCutcheon and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on December 10, 1999. Featuring Whitney Houston, Mario Piu, Jamiroquai, Celine Dion, Artful Dodger feat Craig David, Atomic Kitten and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Paul Burnett presents an edition from May 31, 1979, with performances by Quantum Jump, Chas & Dave, Sparks, Fischer-Z, Peaches & Herb, Voyager, Blondie and Hot Chocolate. Plus, dance sequences by Legs & Co

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Gary Davies present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 1 June 1989. Featuring Sinitta, Fuzzbox and Neneh Cherry

programme.title: Aretha Franklin at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of archive appearances on the BBC by Aretha Franklin. Featuring a stunning 2015 performance of You Make Me Feel (Like a Natural Woman) that had the then US president Barack Obama in tears, plus gems from the 1970s when Aretha dropped in to be the guest star on shows hosted by Cliff Richard and Lulu. These songs - and her voice - demonstrate why she remains considered as the ultimate queen of soul

programme.title: Aretha Franklin in Amsterdam 1968
programme.desc: A performance by the American soul singer recorded at the Royal Concertgebouw in Amsterdam on Sunday April 28,1968. Aretha sings a selection of hits, including Respect, Chain of Fools and Satisfaction

programme.title: Dolly Parton: Here I Am
programme.desc: A profile of the American country star, charting the extraordinary life and music of the singer and songwriter. The documentary takes the audience on a musical journey through some of her greatest songs, including The Bridge, I Will Always Love You, and 9 to 5, and there is footage of Dolly celebrating the 50th anniversary of her first performance at the Grand Ole Opry. The film also features access to the singer herself, as well as collaborators from throughout her career

programme.title: Sings the Great American Songbook
programme.desc: A compilation of archive performances of hits composed by American songwriters including Cole Porter, Irving Berlin, Johnny Mercer and Harold Arlen. Featuring music by Shirley Bassey, Bryan Ferry, Captain Sensible, Jamie Cullum and Florence and the Machine

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs host an edition of the music hall programme first broadcast in March 1976, with Ken Dodd, Sheila Steafal, Valerie Masterson, Johnny Hart and members of the Players' Theatre, London entertaining an audience at the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on December 10, 1999. Featuring Whitney Houston, Mario Piu, Jamiroquai, Celine Dion, Artful Dodger feat Craig David, Atomic Kitten and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Paul Burnett presents an edition from May 31, 1979, with performances by Quantum Jump, Chas & Dave, Sparks, Fischer-Z, Peaches & Herb, Voyager, Blondie and Hot Chocolate. Plus, dance sequences by Legs & Co

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Gary Davies present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 1 June 1989. Featuring Sinitta, Fuzzbox and Neneh Cherry

programme.title: Aretha Franklin at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of archive appearances on the BBC by Aretha Franklin. Featuring a stunning 2015 performance of You Make Me Feel (Like a Natural Woman) that had the then US president Barack Obama in tears, plus gems from the 1970s when Aretha dropped in to be the guest star on shows hosted by Cliff Richard and Lulu. These songs - and her voice - demonstrate why she remains considered as the ultimate queen of soul

programme.title: Expedition Rhino: The Search for the Last Northern White
programme.desc: With the death of the last captive male, Sudan, the Northern White Rhinoceros was officially declared extinct in 2018. But in recent years, possible sightings from a remote region in the heart of Africa have given conservationists hope. This film follows an international team of experts on an expedition to war-torn South Sudan in search of the northern white rhinoceros. An animal native to this unexplored and dangerous region in Africa, the team is taking a huge risk to realise the dream that a lost population of these incredible beasts still exists

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs host an edition of the music hall programme first broadcast in March 1976, with Ken Dodd, Sheila Steafal, Valerie Masterson, Johnny Hart and members of the Players' Theatre, London entertaining an audience at the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye

programme.title: Vermiglio (2024)
programme.desc: In 1944, a Sicilian deserter arrives in a remote Alpine village and has a profound effect on the quiet, everyday lives of the devout Graziadei family, especially the daughters. Italian drama, starring Giuseppe De Domenico, Tommaso Ragno, Roberta Rovelli and Martina Scrinzi. In Italian

programme.title: Butterflies
programme.desc: Middle-aged housewife Ria worries that life is passing her by and wonders if things will ever perk up. However, she is consumed with guilt on meeting new love interest Leonard, a lonely executive with time on his hands. First episode of Carla Lane's comedy, starring Wendy Craig and Geoffrey Palmer. First aired in 1978

programme.title: Artsnight
programme.desc: In rarely seen interviews writer Carla Lane discusses her life and work, while her son Carl offers personal insights into his mother's career and legacy. With contributions from the Liver Birds - Polly James and Nerys Hughes - and Geoffrey Palmer, the long-suffering husband to Ria in Butterflies

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye

But in recent years, possible sightings from a remote region in the heart of Africa have given conservationists hope. This film follows an international team of experts on an expedition to war-torn South Sudan in search of the northern white rhinoceros. An animal native to this unexplored and dangerous region in Africa, the team is taking a huge risk to realise the dream that a lost population of these incredible beasts still exists

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Characterful cottages in both Bridgend and Norfolk are bought by people who didn't originally intend to bid on them, while a Staffordshire building plot just keeps on giving and giving. Presented by Dion Dublin, Tommy Walsh and Martin Roberts

programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from an antiques fair in Kingston upon Thames where a sisters Julie and Debbie take on newlyweds Jack and Amanda under the watchful eye of experts Jonathan Pratt and Kate Bliss

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: The winery's finances depend on caring for a turkey and fending off frost. Rival Marissa's new vintner gets a mixed reception

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple from Berkshire who are looking for a fresh start in a new home in the Herefordshire countryside, following a life-threatening, life-changing event

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Diarmuid Gavin are in Oxford to help Adam, Vaneesha and her father, Graham, transform their two triangular, mirror-image gardens each with a patio and a solitary shed into a family-friendly space, with a budget of £6,000

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp hosts the game show, which sees Debbie McGee, Katie McGlynn, John Whaite, and Rhys Stephenson taking it in turns to cross the floor by only stepping on the correct answers - and doing their best to avoid the wrong ones. For the final crossing, those who have been successful join forces for a chance to take home the money for their chosen charities

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Ria Lina joins Alexander Armstrong for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop
programme.desc: Sara Cox meets Julia, a jazz singer with powerful memories of Reading's Central Club - a place of music, domino tournaments and Caribbean pride, where her father was a local legend. With the building now abandoned, Ethan Goodbody faces the challenge of reviving its spirit in miniature. His intention is to bring back the stage, the iconic mural and the heartbeat of a vanished community

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: In their final challenge, the amateur cooks serve a three-course menu that delivers in presentation, taste and originality, hoping they will have done enough to impress Anna Haugh and Grace Dent and be crowned MasterChef Champion 2026. This will no doubt be a tight race as the finalists draw out every ounce of culinary know-how in the hope of coming out on top

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: David Tennant hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with politician and journalist Michael Gove and comedian Chloe Petts joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: After accidentally picking up one of Cathy's novels, Agnes discovers she has a surprising fondness for steamy fiction. She then gatecrashes the next group meeting of Cathy's book group to share her insights on the genre, - mortifying her daughter in the process. Comedy, co-written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Alex's Old People Day social event does not go to plan. Bruce mistakenly saves a lady who has a 'do not resuscitate' order in place. Matt gets on better with the elderly than Ron

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Bruce thinks Matt's home-made juice has great business potential. Alex suspects Val is lying about being on jury duty. Ron gets in an argument with another doctor while his son Michael is visiting

programme.title: Bodies Bodies Bodies (2022)
programme.desc: When Sophie and new girlfriend Bee attend a party at a remote family mansion, drugs and alcohol flow. Bee unknowingly devours a dope-spiked cake, and tension between revellers explodes in the darkened house during a game of Bodies Bodies Bodies. However, one member of the party soon plays a victim for real. Horror comedy, starring Amandla Stenberg, Maria Bakalova and Rachel Sennott

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall, Rick Stein, Ching-He Huang, Gennaro Contaldo and Andi Oliver, and by special guests Liz Carr and Chris Stark, to celebrate the show's 20th anniversary. Drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are memorable moments from the BBC food archive

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Mrs Brown's Boys actress Eilish O'Carroll (Winnie McGoogan) joins Anna to explore their native Dublin City. They pay a visit to the iconic Guinness factory, where brewing began in 1759. Inspired by the visit, Anna cooks with her mum, evoking childhood memories. Eilish also takes Anna to Moore Street, where traditional markets mix with global street food. The pair later cook a dish inspired by the city's diverse flavours

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three sets of developers seek to squeeze every bit of space out of their new properties in Northampton, Leeds and Coventry

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian presents the latest contest from the Great Wetherby Racecourse Antiques Fair in West Yorkshire. Helped by experts Caroline Hawley and Colin Young, the red and the blue teams go head to head to see who can buy the most profitable items to sell at auction. Meanwhile, Danny has an intriguing mystery item to perplex fairgoers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A smashed-up wardrobe poses a carpentry conundrum for Bruce Kenneth. Can he hammer, saw and smash his way to perfection?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple who are looking for a new home in the countryside of West Sussex after 15 years of living and working in Dubai

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Florence Headlam are in Egham to transform an overgrown and unloved plot into an Islamic-themed garden that echoes the owners' trips to the Middle East

programme.sub-title: Weighing Scales, Mini Lifebelts, African Birthing Chairs
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as five sellers aim to get the best price for their items, including weighing scales, miniature lifebelts, lightning rods, vases and African birthing chairs

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes quiz, with Olympian Greg Rutherford, comedian and actor Sanjeev Kohli, presenter and writer Janet Ellis and actor Louisa Lytton playing to win money for their chosen charities. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money with each correct answer

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Chris McCausland, Emmett J Scanlan, Melvin Odoom, Sue Perkins, Grace Dent and Vanessa Williams

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a comedy edition of the star-studded general knowledge quiz. Olga Koch, Chloe Petts, Arabella Weir, Jon Culshaw, Ted Robbins, Justin Moorhouse, Neil Delamere and Loyiso Gola try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Celebrity edition of the quiz, hosted by Romesh Ranganathan. Mel B, Eamonn Holmes, Shobna Gulati, Denis Lawson, Arielle Free, Briony May Williams, Nick Pickard and Rob Bent are the contestants battling it out to be crowned the strongest link and will take home the prize for their charity

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Flynn tries to secure treatment to abused soldier Rory, and confides in Stevie that the case has disturbing parallels with a tragic incident from his own military service. But Rory may be too scared to allow himself to be helped. Jodie discovers a possible link between cancer patients and the Wyvern Hill Estate, and Rash crosses a line to assuage his guilt

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe feels liberated at a Gods and Monsters party hosted by eccentric local expats Flick and James, while Dan spirals into a dark place back at the villa. News of their separation destabilises each of the friends as the party takes unexpected turns. Drama, starring Jessica Raine

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Blade Runner 2049 (2017)
programme.desc: An android-hunter working for the Los Angeles Police Department discovers a long-buried secret, which leads him to track down former detective Rick Deckard, who's been missing for 30 years and is linked to the case. Denis Villeneuve's sci-fi thriller, the sequel to Ridley Scott's 1982 classic, starring Ryan Gosling, Harrison Ford, Ana de Armas and Sylvia Hoeks

programme.title: Planet Sex with Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: In Palm Springs, Cara parties at the biggest lesbian festival in the world - The Dinah Shore Weekender, where she describes how freeing it feels to be accepted as herself. Unpicking the various types of sexual orientation, Cara heads to London to talk to psychologists and sexologists, and takes part in an experiment at a sex laboratory in the Netherlands to learn about the spectrum of sexuality

programme.title: Dubai Hustle
programme.desc: Natasha tries to swipe Eddie's biggest client and Jessica poses up a storm in a lavish house. Adam receives a surprising update from his client

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Alexandra Palace in north London, venue of the BBC's first ever television broadcast, stages the south of England heat, as comics Blank Peng, Fab Goualin, Sharifa Butterfly, Sydney May and Evaldas Karosas battle it out to book their place in this year's grand final showdown. Host Sara Pascoe is joined by stand-up comics Catherine Bohart and Michael Odewale, who appear alongside Fatiha El-Ghorri on the panel of judges

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Things do not go to plan when Pip gives evidence in court. Connor takes part in a risky set-up in the hope of extracting information from one of Pip's old foes - and while it doesn't reveal anything about the search for Jamie it does turn up something unexpected about Cara, leading to a heated confrontation between her and Pip

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: A sinister message sends Pip, Ravi and Connor to a remote location where a disturbing piece of evidence reveals they are retracing Jamie's steps. An unexpected encounter leaves Pip questioning whether she was right to discount Max as her original suspect. Max shocks everyone with his testimony in court, throwing the proceedings into further uncertainty

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Slovenia in search of the nose-horned viper and also hopes to track down a bear and goes deep into a cave system to find a dragon

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A game show contestant is stabbed during filming of the series's grand finale. In a baffling turn of events, the victim is murdered in mid-air while hurtling down a zip line. As various members of the TV crew come under suspicion, Mervin surprises his colleagues by signing a contract to prolong his stay on Saint Marie. However, he and Selwyn clash when it becomes clear Mervin has an ulterior motive for extending his visit. It also turns out that Selwyn has secrets of his own. Drama, starring Don Gilet and Don Warrington

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Things do not go to plan when Pip gives evidence in court. Connor takes part in a risky set-up in the hope of extracting information from one of Pip's old foes - and while it doesn't reveal anything about the search for Jamie it does turn up something unexpected about Cara, leading to a heated confrontation between her and Pip

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: A sinister message sends Pip, Ravi and Connor to a remote location where a disturbing piece of evidence reveals they are retracing Jamie's steps. An unexpected encounter leaves Pip questioning whether she was right to discount Max as her original suspect. Max shocks everyone with his testimony in court, throwing the proceedings into further uncertainty

programme.title: Planet Sex with Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Documentary in which Cara explores the biggest questions about human sexuality. She begins by asking why men appear to be having more fulfilling sex than women. She uncovers the science behind orgasms in Germany, travels to Japan to make 'vagina art', takes a masturbation class in New York and attends a women-only sex party in LA

programme.title: Bodies Bodies Bodies (2022)
programme.desc: When Sophie and new girlfriend Bee attend a party at a remote family mansion, drugs and alcohol flow. Bee unknowingly devours a dope-spiked cake, and tension between revellers explodes in the darkened house during a game of Bodies Bodies Bodies. However, one member of the party soon plays a victim for real. Horror comedy, starring Amandla Stenberg, Maria Bakalova and Rachel Sennott

programme.title: Love in the Flesh
programme.desc: Zara McDermott presents a dating show exploring what happens when couples who have formed relationships through apps and social media finally meet face to face

programme.title: Love in the Flesh
programme.desc: All five couples go on their first dates with no screens or filters to hide behind. Zara McDermott announces that there are two private suites and that it's up to the group to decide who should spend the night in them and why. Tension builds when one of the housemates reveals her head has been turned by another of the men in the beach house, and her online partner is left feeling crushed

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The Boiler Shop arts and performance space in Newcastle is the venue for the fourth heat, which sees comics Charlie Ashton, Murph, Zoe Cousin, Caira O'Connor and Shugufta Choudhry battling it out to book their place in this year's grand final in Bradford. Amy Gledhill hosts the proceedings and the judges for the night are Teesside local Phil Dunning, former winner Dan Tiernan and head judge Fatiha El-Ghorri

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: A overly dependent young woman's parents die in a car accident, forcing her to fend for herself. It transpires she will inherit the house on condition she completes a list of 'Things You Should Have Done' left by her parents. Chi's highly strung Auntie Karen wants nothing to do with her 'pest of a niece', but very much wants the house she feels is rightfully hers. Offbeat, dysfunctional family sitcom, written by and starring Lucia Keskin, with Selin Hizli

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Chi spectacularly fails a trial period at the pharmacy with Karen, but Dave kindly lets her shadow him on his delivery rounds. On a drop-off to a retirement home Chi finds her tribe - old people. She also falls in love with Linda, an elderly lady who seems to think Chi is her husband. Dave has a health scare and Karen starts to think she might be the fertility issue. Offbeat, dysfunctional family sitcom, starring Lucia Keskin, Selin Hizli and Daniel Fearn

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An encounter with creatures from a universe with only two dimensions leaves the Doctor trapped in side a rapidly shrinking TARDIS. Clara must take on the creatures alone to protect a group of community service workers as every image on every wall becomes a potentially lethal threat. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: In the Forest of the Night
programme.desc: One morning, the human race wakes up to find the planet has been invaded by its own trees, as overnight a forest has grown everywhere and taken back the Earth. Clara and Danny have to lead a school trip through the wilderness that has overtaken London, while the Doctor realsies the final days of humanity may have finally arrived. Peter Capaldi stars in an episode by novelist and screenwriter Frank Cottrell Boyce

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny and Miranda are taken into police custody and made to see a therapist - whose assessment technique entails staring at them in stony silence while they grow increasingly nervous and irritable. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart and Patricia Hodge

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The joke-shop owner dreams of the perfect Christmas without her parents and decides to celebrate at home with Stevie, Gary, Clive and Tilly instead. However, a lack of presents, an infuriating encounter at the post office, a run-in with some carol singers and a few arguments in the flat all begin to wear Miranda down - and she may end up back with her mother and father after all. Guest starring Tom Conti, with Miranda Hart. First broadcast in 2010

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Grandad's brother Micky is fresh out of prison and decides to stop by the house for a visit, but his family are soon suspicious when he asks for 5,000 euro to invest in a South African goldmine. Winnie is inspired by Cathy's book to change the trajectory of her life, while the boys have a heart to heart

programme.title: The Nice Guys (2016)
programme.desc: A private detective investigates the murder of a porn star in 1970s Los Angeles, only to run into a hired thug employed to scare him off. However, the mysterious disappearance of a woman connected to the case forces the unlikely duo to work together, leading them to uncover a government conspiracy. Crime comedy, starring Russell Crowe and Ryan Gosling

programme.title: Daisy Maskell: Insomnia and Me
programme.desc: For as long as she can remember, Daisy Maskell has suffered with insomnia, surviving on as little as a couple of hours a sleep a night. In this documentary, the breakfast radio host sets out to uncover the root cause of her problem and take the first steps toward treatment. She meets fellow sufferers to investigate why thousands of young people in the UK are struggling with sleep disorders, and tries a multitude of remedies and treatments in the hope of finally getting a better night's sleep

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz must choose between Donna and Janet when they give him an ultimatum to decide who he wants to be with - but as the deadline approaches, he still has doubts. Elsewhere, Louise worries Scott will run off with a groupie

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Two burglars break into a house, where they come across a man already tied up. Comedy short, written by and starring Stephen Buchanan and Martin Quinn

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An encounter with creatures from a universe with only two dimensions leaves the Doctor trapped in side a rapidly shrinking TARDIS. Clara must take on the creatures alone to protect a group of community service workers as every image on every wall becomes a potentially lethal threat. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: In the Forest of the Night
programme.desc: One morning, the human race wakes up to find the planet has been invaded by its own trees, as overnight a forest has grown everywhere and taken back the Earth. Clara and Danny have to lead a school trip through the wilderness that has overtaken London, while the Doctor realsies the final days of humanity may have finally arrived. Peter Capaldi stars in an episode by novelist and screenwriter Frank Cottrell Boyce

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Maternity Failures: The Fight for Justice
programme.desc: Michael Buchanan reports on the rise in women dying in childbirth in the UK, and an inquiry reviewing the medical records of thousands of births for signs of avoidable harm. The programme features testimonies from mothers who weren't listened to, staff who blew the whistle but were ignored, and parents whose babies' deaths were dismissed as just bad luck

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The quarter-final sees the teams heading to the literary town of Hay-on-Wye in Wales, where they work in pairs to transform two high street shops. One sells guitars, the other arts and crafts, and the aim of the designers is to improve layout and visual merchandising to entice more customers. Alan follows their progress, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by retail expert Ross Bailey to assess the final results

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan joins officers from the Police Service of Northern Ireland as they patrol the streets of Belfast. Dave stumbles upon a drug den. Jade and Adam open up about the mental health scars of being a Peeler and Perri uses pepper spray to bring a violent suspect down

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: It's Jacob's birthday, and everyone notices there's something up with Zoe. She punishes Dan as she focuses on Solomon's big career break. Dan's romantic trip might offer hope, but it triggers deeply buried memories for Zoe. Jacob is trying to hide his age, not helped by Will's mates gatecrashing their night out

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Frances Tophill are in Leicester to help Hector and Claudia transform their blank new-build plot into a Cypriot cooking garden for a growing family. Uneven grass, a sloped area behind the garage and a bare brick wall have left the space difficult to use. The couple dream of a garden reflecting Hector's Cypriot heritage and their shared love of food, with a dedicated outdoor cooking and dining area

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian journey takes him back to Brussels to examine a dark chapter in the country's past. At the Museum of Africa, on the edge of the city, he learns about Belgium's brutal seizure of the Congo under King Leopold II and a grand exhibition to celebrate his colonial success. Travelling south into French-speaking Wallonia, Michael visits the site of the Battle of Waterloo, one of Britain's most famous military victories

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From a windswept island chapel battered by the North Sea to a quiet Welsh farmhouse that shaped the future of a language, this episode travels to two remote National Trust sites whose powerful stories have inspired centuries of pilgrims - and reveals the delicate conservation work protecting their legacy. Toby Jones narrates

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, who answer questions on the specialist subjects of film adaptations of Shakespeare tragedies, the Great Fire of London, the career of John Barnes, and The Lord of the Rings by JRR Tolkien, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team travels to north London to help out a family of five. In one day, Tommy Walsh, Maxine Dwyer and Asher Edwards set out to transform the living room from an overrun play area into a space fit for all the family. While upstairs they take on the challenge of creating a multifunctional home office and guest room

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: With the sun shining and the flowers blooming, summer is a season of splendid abundance, and the long hours of daylight make life burst out in a riot of activity. However, in certain regions there can be sweltering heat. As this documentary reveals, for a range of species, from sneaky ring-tailed lemurs to battling ibex and penguins to colour-changing lizards, summer is a time when the living is not always easy. Narrated by Andrew Scott

programme.title: Weather Watchers with Barra Best
programme.desc: The weather presenter and journalist examines how climate shapes everyone's lives and looks at the activities that it enables people to participate in

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges ahead of Friday's final

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez make a final push for victory on double points Friday before the winner picks up that trophy

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian journey takes him back to Brussels to examine a dark chapter in the country's past. At the Museum of Africa, on the edge of the city, he learns about Belgium's brutal seizure of the Congo under King Leopold II and a grand exhibition to celebrate his colonial success. Travelling south into French-speaking Wallonia, Michael visits the site of the Battle of Waterloo, one of Britain's most famous military victories

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his Belgian journey, Michael Portillo travels to Namur, capital of Wallonia, to discover a citadel dating back to the 10th century. He learns about the traditional sport of stilt jousting, still popular in the city today, and enjoys an exciting battle between the two historic teams

programme.title: Today at the Test
programme.sub-title: England v New Zealand: First Test Day Two
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from the second day of the First Test in the three-match series, held at Lord's. The last time these two sides met in this country was right at the start of Ben Stokes and Brendon McCullum's tenure as England captain and coach in 2022. They played in a three-match series that saw England make the perfect start, as they claimed a 3-0 victory

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Roses are stealing the spotlight at Longmeadow, and Monty Don is celebrating all the full, head-turning bloom. Ashley Edwards visits Benton End in Suffolk, a place of artistic vision and horticultural legacy. And in Penarth, Sue Kent uncovers a rich mix of gardens, personalities and inspiration

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From samurai armour to rare wildlife, this episode explores remarkable collections and the experts preserving them. At Snowshill Manor in the Cotswolds, Charles Paget Wade's 22,000 curiosities include rare Japanese armour, with a child's samurai suit restored for exhibition at the British Museum. And at Calke Abbey in Derbyshire, specialists protect historic taxidermy collections

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Two police officers always have time to get to know each other while working on the night shift together. However, PC Thompson's previous partner died in a brutal attack less than a month previously, the forensic evidence has gone missing and PCSO Varney is beginning to ask a lot of awkward questions. The final instalment of the fifth series of the anthology of darkly comic tales, written by and starring The League of Gentlemen and Psychoville's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Rebecca Callard and Malik Ibheis

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Bring Them Down (2024)
programme.desc: An Irish shepherding family thrust into battle on several fronts: internal strife, hostility within the family, rivalry with another farmer. Drama, starring Christopher Abbott, Barry Keoghan and Colm Meaney

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals reach their end, and four of the five remaining cooks will eventually make it through to Finals Week. Anna Haugh and Grace Dent have two demanding tasks up their sleeves, and will want to see glimpses of championship-winning food in all the plates delivered. The judges have invited pastry chef Philip Khoury, and the cooks are asked to recreate one of his signature desserts

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget attempts to make all her Piri friends' secret hopes and dreams come true, but gets them the wrong way round, with funny and very unexpected consequences

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Big Lizard steals Dad's special plant from the ship - and just in time

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy finds a box full of magic props but won't share, upsetting Spicy who wants to be a magician too

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The tired heroes try to find out and stop whatever is making the loud wailing noise that has kept the residents of the forest up all night

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy wants to watch TV, but the Lemmings want to have fun playing music

programme.title: Duck & Frog
programme.desc: Duck realises that dogs are a duck?s best friend when he walks a poodle. But when Frog realises a picture with a poodle could mean fame, he goes all out to snap the dog for himself

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: An encounter with a gang of Dragon Tamers leads Liko and Roy to an unexpected reunion

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: The brothers try to survive Britain's most haunted castle and four terrifying Gladiators as they attempt to avoid the tricks and collect the treats

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.sub-title: Hammer, Nitro, Fire, Phantom v the Gym Rematch!
programme.desc: Hammer, Nitro, Fire and Phantom flex their prank powers in the gym, but only one can win. Will doughnut dares, motivational mayhem or haunted gym bikes cause an early tap out?

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Football World Cup Takeover with SV2 and Manny
programme.desc: With a football World Cup takeover featuring social media stars SV2 and Manny

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Roses are stealing the spotlight at Longmeadow, and Monty Don is celebrating all the full, head-turning bloom. Ashley Edwards visits Benton End in Suffolk, a place of artistic vision and horticultural legacy. And in Penarth, Sue Kent uncovers a rich mix of gardens, personalities and inspiration

programme.title: Great Expectations (1946)
programme.desc: An orphan falls in love with the cold-hearted adopted daughter of an eccentric old woman. A mysterious benefactor gives him the chance to rise through London's high society, but in the process he forgets his humble roots and is not prepared for the truth about the girl he loves and the stranger funding his new life. Drama, based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring John Mills, Jean Simmons, Finlay Currie and Alec Guinness

programme.title: The Wooden Horse (1950)
programme.desc: British PoWs in a German camp try to escape their captors by taking up gymnastics, and using a vaulting horse to conceal the tunnel they are digging. However, even if they manage to escape the camp, the journey to neutral territory will not be an easy one. Fact-based Second World War drama, starring Leo Genn, David Tomlinson, Anthony Steel and David Greene

programme.title: Ice Station Zebra (1968)
programme.desc: An American atomic submarine commander races against time to reach a Soviet spy satellite that has crash-landed near a British research station in the Arctic - before Russian troops arrive to stake their claim to it. Adventure based on Alistair MacLean's novel, starring Rock Hudson, Ernest Borgnine, Jim Brown, Tony Bill and Patrick McGoohan

programme.title: Super League Live
programme.desc: Wakefield Trinity v Hull Kingston Rovers (Kick-off 5.30pm). All the action from the match at DIY Kitchens Stadium. Rovers are the reigning champions, but Wakefield claimed a 28-12 victory when the teams last met here in in the penultimate round of regular-season fixtures in 2025, with four different players scoring tries for the victors, including Max Jowitt, who also kicked six goals

programme.title: The Game - The Story of Hurling
programme.desc: A journey through the history of hurling and what it means to Northern Ireland as a whole, looking first at the groundwork on which the sport was founded

programme.title: Suzie Lee's Great Food Made Simple
programme.desc: Suzie Lee Arbuthnot creates an easy poke bowl

programme.title: Today at the Test
programme.sub-title: England v New Zealand: First Test Day Three
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day three of the First Test in the three-match series, held at Lord's. Ben Stokes' side have won two of the last three Test series against New Zealand, as well as winning six of their last eight matches against them. The last time these two teams met in this format, England claimed a 2-1 series victory in New Zealand, as they won the first two matches by eight wickets and 323 runs respectively

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Scotland v Bolivia (Kick-off 9.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Sports Illustrated Stadium in Harrison, New Jersey. This is Scotland's final warm-up match before they face Haiti in their opening match of the FIFA World Cup on 13th June in Foxborough. This contest is likely to serve as a good test, as the Bolivians only missed out on the finals themselves after losing out to Iraq in the play-offs, having finished one place outside the automatic qualifying places in the notoriously difficult CONMEBOL group

programme.title: Weather Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood and Tomasz Schafernaker present a selection of performances of weather-related songs from the archives, including Bill Withers singing Ain't No Sunshine When She's Gone and Why Does It Always Rain on Me? By Travis

programme.title: Paris, 13th District (2021)
programme.desc: The lives of four young people intertwine as they navigate love, sex and romance in a modern city. Romance, starring Lucie Zhang and Makita Samba. In French, Mandarin and English

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: A runner is found dead on the set of a Welsh TV drama. John helps Janie to investigate, and is struck by how much of an outsider he feels in what should be his natural home - especially whern he runs into an old co-star. Janie befriends the show's leading actor, leaving John concerned that she will not be able to put her personal connection aside and stay open to all suspects. Guest starring Jim Howick (Ghosts)

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the devastating defeat at Wembley, Southgate's guilt weighs heavily on his conscience. When the fans then turn on the manager, accusing him of prioritising politics over performance, he begins to doubt himself ahead of a controversial World Cup in Qatar. Drama, starring Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: Paranormal Survivor
programme.desc: A woman's nephew is attacked by something horrific, a man takes a job at a restaurant with a resident ghost and a woman is stalked by an obsessive spirit with a violent streak

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In 2016, multiple witnesses in and around the village of Pentyrch, south Wales, saw a vast triangular UFO appearing in the night sky, ejecting smaller red and green craft before engaging in a battle with the RAF, culminating in its apparent shooting down. The presenter and astrophysicist Sarah Cruddas examine video evidence of the alleged UFO crash site and consider natural phenomena before speaking to UFO investigator Gari Jones from the Swansea UFO Network, who has failed to get a clear explanation from the military of these events

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Alien Pilots, Carolina Lights and Stone-Cold Animals
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things 'caught on film'. This episode features footage purportedly of alien pilots in a UFO cockpit. Plus, the claims that a Australian man stumbled upon an alien corpse on the pavement

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The staff receive complaints from customers that a staff member has been letting customers cut in line in exchange for a tip. They later find out that Anton, who works the pawn desk, is responsible

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth swaps the workstations for two members of his team. However, Les becomes angered at the situation, feeling that the arrangement was good enough in the first place

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Jeff calls in sick, leading to Seth to order everyone to stay away from his desk in any circumstances. He then finds Bobby J in the banned area, polishing a ring for a customer

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team travel around the country assessing items brought in by members of the public, who either take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team value antiques in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire. Helen Gardiner examines some Wade pottery pigs, while David Hakeney looks at a Royal Doulton jug and bowl. Plus, Stewart Hofgartner considers buying a Huntley & Palmers biscuit tin

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick gets a visit from The Boss when a Bruce Springsteen tour jacket rocks into the store. Meanwhile, Corey checks out some motorcycle art

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick checks out a bass guitar signed by Led Zeppelin's John Paul Jones and The Who's John Entwistle

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycler Sarah Peterson's creativity is tested with a tired set of cane chairs, while robot maker Mark Haig's challenge is to come up with a new use for an old video projector. For his own project, presenter Jay Blades gets crafty with a collection of discarded windows

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Hoping to uncover a wealth of treasures, the buyers travel to the town of Aurora, where Roy soon spots a hidden gem that may give him the upper hand

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: The buyers visit Orangeville, Ontario, where Ursula is determined to prove she is a major player, while Paul and Bogart uncover an unusual item

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Brampton becomes a battleground as Roy blasts the buyers with rude and crude comments, while Cindy does her best to throw Ursula off her game

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: In Orangeville, the buyers resort to mind games to intimidate the competition. Roy manages to distract Cindy, but gets caught up in Ursula's charm

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Roy uncovers a potentially lucrative item, before Bogart finds a locker that he knows a thing or two about

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.sub-title: Ain't No Locker Like a Birthday Locker
programme.desc: Competition between the auction specialists comes to a head on Rick's birthday when the buyers find several intriguing items

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Roy arrives at an auction incognito, hoping it will improve his luck, but the other bidders are unsure about the newcomer

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Ursula makes a large bid to win a fashion locker in Rick and Cindy's home town, and hopes the clothes will be worth the gamble

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A rescue buoy from the set of Baywatch signed by David Hasselhoff is brought in, as well as two flintlock blunderbuss pistols. Rick and Corey check out a six-person solar bike

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore sees what people are about to throw away at Dunbar Recycling Centre, rescuing a set of drawers, an unwanted computer desk and an old accordion case for transformation

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Investigators find evidence suggesting that ancient aliens saved humanity from great floods, and contemplate whether mankind may one day need to rely on the intervention of extra-terrestrials again

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas scrutinise an astonishing case that took place in November 2018 off the west coast of Ireland, in which multiple pilots saw bizarre lights through their cockpit windows. Ted Roe, Director of Research at the National Aviation Reporting Centre shares extraordinary insights into the sighting, while an ex-Nato air traffic controller - under the cover of anonymity - acts as a whistle-blower, spilling more secrets of the sky and the shocking lengths that governments are prepared to go to hide the truth

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Ursula tries her best to put up with her bossy older sister, Rick takes the reins, Roy calls the cops, and Paul and Bogart lose their tempers

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: All the buyers get burned on the hottest auction day of the year, as temperatures and tempers reach boiling point

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycler Sarah Peterson's creativity is tested with a tired set of cane chairs, while robot maker Mark Haig's challenge is to come up with a new use for an old video projector. For his own project, presenter Jay Blades gets crafty with a collection of discarded windows

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team arrive in Wood Green, north London, to help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. Among the items catching the eyes of expert antiques dealers Jan Keyne, David Ford, Ian Towning and Mark Stevens are an Atlas clock, some Crown Derby cutlery and a large amount of gold

programme.title: WWE's Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Unforgettable WWE moments, with the spotlight on Mark William Calaway, better known by his ring name of The Undertaker

programme.title: WWE's Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Unforgettable WWE moments focusing on the Survivor Series of the 2000s

programme.title: The UnBelievable with Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Improbable yet true-life tales of surviving the most extraordinary conditions. Examples include people free falling 30,000ft without a parachute or being buried alive

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: During the golden age of piracy, thousands of buccaneers terrorised the high seas and became legends. As the veteran actor reveals. their exploits spawned stories of larger-than-life characters, billion dollar heists, and buried treasure that to this day, has never been found

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath explores creepy dolls and a haunted hearse at Nottingham's macabre museum with Dr Kate Cherrell. Kate visits the National Justice Museum, a 17th-century courthouse and prison that may still house the spirits of former inmates. Meanwhile, Andy heads to one of Britain's oldest pubs, where he learns how an unpaid bar tab contributed to the legend of a cursed galleon. Later, he embarks on a quest to find evidence that Robin Hood, Nottinghamshire's legendary hero, may have been more than just a myth

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas scrutinise an astonishing case that took place in November 2018 off the west coast of Ireland, in which multiple pilots saw bizarre lights through their cockpit windows. Ted Roe, Director of Research at the National Aviation Reporting Centre shares extraordinary insights into the sighting, while an ex-Nato air traffic controller - under the cover of anonymity - acts as a whistle-blower, spilling more secrets of the sky and the shocking lengths that governments are prepared to go to hide the truth

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Mystery Sea Beast, The Earth Is Alive and Shapeshifting UFO
programme.desc: A mysterious sea beast seen from the shore of a small seaside town in the UK. Plus, the notion that a field of massive mysterious jars were made by giants. Tony Harris and his team of experts examine the clues and make a determination about the validity of what they are being told

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les sells a valuable Breitling watch for $42,000 to a trusted watch collector that he dealt with many times before. His customer does not have the funds, so Les gives him the merchandise in exchange for an IOU. Seth has grave misgivings about the deal, but Les still has trust in the customer

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les makes a deal to get the others off his back, but when some interesting animals come through the door, it might be too much for him to resist

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: With over 40,000 wristbands to sell, stored in 126 boxes, Les decides to sell them one by one, starting at $2 apiece. Seth and Ashley remain sceptical of the idea

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Expert and presenter David Dickinson presides as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead?

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: The buyers visit Orangeville, Ontario, where Ursula is determined to prove she is a major player, while Paul and Bogart uncover an unusual item

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Brampton becomes a battleground as Roy blasts the buyers with rude and crude comments, while Cindy does her best to throw Ursula off her game

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: In Orangeville, the buyers resort to mind games to intimidate the competition. Roy manages to distract Cindy, but gets caught up in Ursula's charm

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Roy uncovers a potentially lucrative item, before Bogart finds a locker that he knows a thing or two about

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Events that are so destructive, so astounding, they must be directed by the hand of the Almighty

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: During the golden age of piracy, thousands of buccaneers terrorised the high seas and became legends. As the veteran actor reveals. their exploits spawned stories of larger-than-life characters, billion dollar heists, and buried treasure that to this day, has never been found

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Looking at the origin of superstitions surrounding numbers and why so many people are fearful of certain numbers, like the number 13. The fear is so disturbing that hotels skip the 13th floor and airplanes the 13th row

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The patterns in fields, which appear overnight and without any warning. What are crop circles, why are they there and what could they possibly signify? Do they come from other worlds?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The Renaissance was a time of great enlightenment throughout Europe, advances were made in both science and the arts. Many paintings have a mysterious common feature, what some would call today a UFO

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The mystery surrounding a mass produced series of paintings of crying children, a popular artwork in many houses in the 1980s

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Congressional UFOs, New Nessie and Zombie Fish
programme.desc: An examination of a video recently presented to Congress by the UN Navy that might prove once and for all whether UFOs really exist. Plus, alleged Loch Ness Monster footage that offers the most compelling proof of the creature's existence in years. Marine scientists also discover a mysterious 'Yellow Brick Road' at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Monster in the Attic, Houston UFO and Utah Bigfoot
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things 'caught on film'. This episode features a video showing a strange formation of flickering lights and a mysterious humanoid creature caught on camera in an abandoned attic

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Chupacabra, Greatest UFO Photo Ever and Mars Sounds
programme.desc: An examination of a recent caught-on-camera sighting of Chupacabra outside an Amarillo Zoo. Plus, whether a photograph taken over Costa Rica the greatest UFO picture ever taken

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Satellite images reveal how El Chapo escaped from a high security prison in Mexico. Plus, a giant black stain appears in a war-torn area of Iraq

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A giant letter Z is carved into a Mexican hillside evoking the mark of a legendary outlaw. Plus, a strange sighting in Germany points to secrets from the Cold War

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist encounters strange symbols scratched into the Kazakhstan Desert. Plus, a lake in the Brazilian rain forest turns bright pink

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: The Empress of Britain, an enormous, palatial ocean liner that also doubled up as a fabulous first-class cruise ship for the super wealthy. Her fame and appeal was short-lived after the Empress of Britain was called up for war duty

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: The fate of the Ile de France. The luxury ocean liner was regarded as the pride of France, after saving many lives in the Second World War and then emerging from the conflict. Just before being scrapped, the ship was used as a floating prop for the 1960 Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer disaster film The Last Voyage

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler

programme.title: Hitler's Handmaidens
programme.desc: The women who turned Hitler from a rabble-rouser to a politician, training him in the methods of high society to launch him to power. These females helped pioneer the Nazi Party - and voted him in. However, they were soon left behind once he achieved supreme authority

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Sex Life
programme.desc: The myriad of rumours, theories and disputed historical accounts of Adolf Hitler's sexual psychology, considering how it integrated into his overall twisted psyche. This series addresses various time periods of Hitler's alleged and proven sex life and explores the role sex played in shaping Hitler's behaviour

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath returns to Worcestershire, where he pays tribute at the grave of his childhood rock hero and reconnects with Jayne Harrison. Later, Andy encounters a treasure hunter inspired by Indiana Jones and embraces the vibrant traditions of May Day Morris dancing

programme.title: What on Earth?

Starring Alan Cumming and David Morrissey

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: Leo suspects someone has been inside his house, and when he has dinner with his ex, Curtis, all his old fears rise up. Clive starts work on Leo's bar on Canal Street. But when he gets assistance from his son, Saul, who gets recognised, it sets into motion a series of events that lead to a shocking conclusion. Drama, starring Alan Cumming and David Morrissey

Day two sees Sarka and Paul lay on a Czech menu, but the massive portions defeat everyone - except the ever-hungry Rory. Finally, it's the turn of John and Sally with their retro 1980s evening. Which couple will win the cash prize of £1,000?

programme.title: Frasier
