programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Ford's Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones

programme.title: Shalako (1968)
programme.desc: A party of European aristocrats on a hunting trip in New Mexico falls foul of marauding Apaches and the treachery of a guide, who abandons the group and makes off with their possessions. However, a US army scout knows of their presence in the territory and sets out to the rescue. Western, with Sean Connery, Brigitte Bardot, Jack Hawkins, Honor Blackman, Woody Strode, Peter Van Eyck and Eric Sykes

programme.title: The Gambler, the Girl and the Gunslinger (2009)
programme.desc: A ranch owner loses half his home in a poker game, meaning he now has to share the property with his rival gambler. The pair struggle to cope with living and working together, and when they both take a liking to the same woman, the conflict only gets worse. Comedy Western, starring Dean Cain, James Tupper and Allison Hossack

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: A dancer confined to a hospital bed has a recurring dream about Room 22. Guest starring Barbara Nichols and Fred Wayne

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: The crew of a jetliner en route to New York start travelling backward and forward in time

programme.title: Uncommon Valor (1983)
programme.desc: A retired American colonel, frustrated by a decade of wading through red tape, takes the law into his own hands and recruits an elite team of Vietnam veterans to rescue his son from a PoW camp in Laos. Action adventure, starring Gene Hackman, Patrick Swayze, Robert Stack and Fred Ward

programme.title: Drop Zone (1994)
programme.desc: Federal marshal Pete Nessip escorts a convicted computer hacker to a new facility. Events soon spiral out of control and the convict escapes, killing Pete's brother on the way. The tough cop teams up with a professional skydiver to capture the fugitive and avenge his sibling's death. Action thriller, starring Wesley Snipes, Gary Busey, Yancy Butler and Michael Jeter

programme.title: Lucky Number Slevin (2006)
programme.desc: A case of mistaken identity plunges an ordinary man into the middle of a war between crime syndicates. With no means of proving who he really is, he finds himself forced to pay off a friend's debt to an underworld kingpin by assassinating a rival mobster. Thriller, starring Josh Hartnett, Bruce Willis, Lucy Liu, Morgan Freeman and Ben Kingsley

programme.title: A Reason to Live, A Reason to Die (1972)
programme.desc: During the American Civil War, a Union officer faces disgrace for handing over a fort to the Confederates. Determined to regain his honour, he rescues seven men condemned to death and offers them a stay of execution if they help him retake the fort. Spaghetti Western, starring James Coburn and Telly Savalas

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A two-year-old is having a constant seizure and needs to get to hospital urgently; he won't stop fitting and his oxygen levels are falling. A young woman is involved in a road traffic collision with a lorry. A paramedic opens up to a patient about losing a loved one to cancer, and a 91-year-old woman with chest pain and a history of severe heart complaints seeks assistance by calling 999

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Silas works his first ever shift in resus, and is part of the team brought in to treat a biker who is rushed to A&E after riding into a lamppost. A two-year-old boy is treated for injuries resulting from a fall, and his mother shares the story of how her eldest son was recently attacked by a gang

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the night shift in a busy A&E department. A woman with a severe form of autism is brought in, having suffered an injury to one of her fingers. While she is treated, her sister shares stories of their childhood and the challenges their family have faced. An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The campers visit Tyn-yr-Onnen farm on the edge of Snowdonia National Park, where hosts Len and Helen encourage them to embrace farm life, from feeding animals to picking mushrooms

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Lincolnshire, where they meet mother and son Sarah Jane and Josh, who are trying to restore a historic windmill, and Sandy and Andy, who plan to open a glamping site on their family farm

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet author and broadcaster Bob Flowerdew in Norfolk, where Henry eyes up a pinball machine from a damp shed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another quartet of B&B owners battle it out to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money, beginning with Paul Billington and Flora Loizou from Silverwood Lodge in Cambridgeshire. The couple get a frosty response when one guest learns her hosts do not live on-site, while another is left in hysterics by an unexpected discovery

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group sets off for Great Yarmouth to stay in Sue Dodd's dog-friendly Seashells Guesthouse. Spirits run high with a trip to the beach and a game of bingo, but Nancy's complaints over dog hairs on the sheets and smoking in the rooms bring down the light-hearted mood during dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: American ex-banker Nancy Hampson hosts at the Old Court Guesthouse in Bath, but her vocal criticisms of the week's first two establishments have made her no friends among her fellow competitors. Paul Billington is annoyed that the bed he and wife Flora Loizou are given is not big enough, but is more impressed when taken for food at a quirky local restaurant

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests stay with Caroline Scott, the owner of the Dean B&B near Longniddry, East Lothian The picturesque house and breathtaking views across the Firth of Forth win everyone over, but underwear in the drawers, cobwebs on the curtains and a messy breakfast service seriously hamper the host's chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Sue from Seashells in Great Yarmouth confronts Nancy Hampson about the controversial topic of smoking in bedrooms, while Paul Billington and Flora Loizou are held to account about their low mark for the Old Court Guesthouse

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant 49-year-old performing arts teacher Helen gets the party started in Sheffield. Her cooking doesn't go to plan, but she certainly delivers on the entertainment, on a whirlwind evening featuring trumping, spies and a dessert that goes with a bang

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Design and technology teacher Claire hosts the second day of the competition in Sheffield, and hopes her Asian-inspired menu with a spot of clay model-making will impress her fellow diners enough to net her the points she'll need to secure the cash prize. However, former chef Simon Hollings proves difficult to faze, and the eccentric Helen irritates the other contestants with another audacious display at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Service administrator Monika hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Sheffield. She treats her guests to a traditional Bulgarian night inspired by her homeland, in the hope of impressing them enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, some of the guests are unsure about her Bulgarian delicacies, and the host must fight for control when the two teachers go head to head and when Simon makes sexist jokes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: After tensions arose between Claire and Helen on Monika's night, 1980s musician Ivor is determined to serve up some fun on his evening in Sheffield. He stages an '80s extravaganza, including a live performance from his new romantic band My Pierrot Dolls

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former chef Simon hosts the final meal in Sheffield, but is unlikely to get an easy ride from his guests following all the criticism he has dished out at the earlier soirees. He also has to contend with the feud that's brewing between Claire and Helen, and when things boil over between the women, Monika doesn't know where to hide

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Wharfe House in Wetherby, run by B&B newcomer Pauline Cox, who's joined by her best friend Claire Smith. Eyebrows are raised when Mike and Karen Hepker arrive from Bath Lodge Castle in a chauffeur-driven car. During a visit to a local vineyard, sparks fly between Mike Hepker and Tash Thorne, who runs The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport with her partner Mike Pearce. Having only just opened, Pauline has a house full for the very first time. The narrow antique beds cause concerns and Jenny and Clive Aylett from Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk are first to criticise breakfast. When it comes to feedback, Pauline's left deflated by her sausage scores - and Karen and Mike Hepker vote with their feet

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners visit Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk, run by husband-and-wife duo Clive and Jenny Aylett, but when the pair's rivals arrive, Pauline Cox and Claire Smith have difficulty locating their favourite appliance. Publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne are astonished to discover they cannot lock their room's door. In the morning, some of the guest make awkward requests to change their breakfast orders, causing professional chef Clive to experience a meltdown in the kitchen that leads to a fundamental error

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Bath Lodge Castle in Somerset for the third day of the competition, where they are wowed by Karen and Mike Hepker's luxury castle experience. Although the guests are disappointed at the bathroom fixtures and fittings, they are given the chance to vent their emotions during an afternoon of painting in the castle grounds. Plus, tensions rise between Mike and Tash at dinner, and harsh comments at feedback lead the hosts to suspect game playing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport, Hampshire, run by publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne. Karen Hepker is disappointed with the honeymoon suite, while Clive and Jenny Aylett find a dubious looking splash guard in the shower. A boat tour backfires when Jenny struggles with her water phobia and at dinner Karen takes the opportunity to tell the others exactly what she thinks of them. But things really kick off when it comes to feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wharfe House hosts Pauline Cox and Claire Smith begin a bitter debate, demanding to know why their beds caused such a sleepless night. Accusations subsequently fly in all directions and walk-outs look on the cards as tensions mount leading to the payments being revealed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps ballet dancer Nicol and his brother Harry get on the property ladder, exploring potential new homes in north-east London. Phil Spencer helps Tinder success story Hiren and Priyanka, who have been searching for a home for a year following a whirlwind romance

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts heat six, as five more players prove they know their Boyzone from their Britney. Who'll come out tops and potentially secure their place in the grand final?

programme.title: Panda
programme.desc: Messina invites Panda and Lola to one of the best restaurants in the region. But in the middle of their meal, a prominent food critic chokes and dies before their eyes. Panda and Lola begin investigating in the kitchen and quickly discover that his death was no accident. In French

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors investigate the underlying medical conditions that may have caused a motorist to crash into a lamppost, while the patient's son and his friend recount the influence she has had on both their lives. A man who fell during the night is treated for possible head trauma, and the father of a three-year-old patient reveals how his stepfather inspired him to be the best parent he can be

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics and the air ambulance rush to save an elderly man who has life-changing injuries following a fall from a ladder. At the same time in Birmingham, a newly qualified paramedic struggles to treat a man who has had three major epileptic fits and is in a delirious and combative state. In Coventry, paramedics Neil Vann and Laura Perkins help a young girl who has passed out and needs urgent medical attention

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A drunk man kicks off at the paramedics in the back of the ambulance. A taxi driver is cut out of his vehicle after a road traffic collision. Back pain leaves a landscape gardener unable to get up off the floor. A 21-year-old woman who hasn't passed urine in over 20 hours is complaining of severe pain in her kidneys, and the construction of a summer house goes badly wrong when sheets of plywood land on the builder's head

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors investigate the underlying medical conditions that may have caused a motorist to crash into a lamppost, while the patient's son and his friend recount the influence she has had on both their lives. A man who fell during the night is treated for possible head trauma, and the father of a three-year-old patient reveals how his stepfather inspired him to be the best parent he can be

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The camping critics' penultimate stop is at Mynydd Derwydd, a wild camping site on the edge of Clocaenog Forest in south-west Wales. With 250 acres of stunning scenery to explore, but next to no facilities, campsite owner Llyr tries to win them over with a bushcraft skills workshop, a lamb barbecue and a breakfast delivery of fresh farm produce

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by husband and wife Ken and Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sandy and Andy dream of eventually retiring to the lakes and mountains of Lazio in Italy, but for now want to use a property there to share holidays with their sizable family of five children and six grandchildren. Danni Menzies works with a budget of £70,000 to hunt out a traditional-style house with plenty of space and a big enough garden to grow fruit and vegetables

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Brannie and Paul are planning a permanent move to Portugal, and are seeking a three-bed house that offers plenty of privacy but is still walkable to amenities. With a budget of £225,000, Danni Menzies sets out to find the pair the perfect property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Eco-enthusiasts Karen and Peter from Liverpool can't wait to find their dream Spanish holiday home in the stunning region of Tarragona in Catalonia. They want a property that allows them to live off-grid, with enough space to accommodate their planned yoga retreat. Danni Menzies lines up five tempting options for their budget of £180,000, hoping that at least one will meet the couple's needs

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kent couple Alison and Lee are making a permanent move to the stunning countryside of Albox in Almería. They need a garden that's secure enough for their four dogs to roam safely, and the property must be big enough to host their six grown-up children and three grandchildren. Ben Hillman lines up some impressive properties for their budget of £150,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Julie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham, hoping to win over her guests with a no-nonsense menu she can prepare in advance. Joining her are tough-talking prison transport officer Tina, dreadlocked farm-dweller Stephen, superior IT manager David and glamorous beauty-school teacher Ashleigh. Alliances are quickly forged when David and Tina discover they both love a good gossip, before they turn their attention to Stephen when David compares him to a tramp. Julie overcooks her beef, but will her hosting make up for it and bag her the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty teacher Ashleigh throws the second of the week's parties in Co Durham, she hopes a menu of sophisticated dishes - along with a helping of hearts and love - will help her win the cash prize. However, cracks were already beginning to show in the diners' genial facade yesterday, and the hostess realises she must play peacemaker if she is to keep everyone from falling out

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Call centre complaints officer and part-time farmer Stephen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham. He hopes to win the £1,000 prize money by impressing his guests with an evening on his farm, featuring a drinks reception and rustic cuisine. However, self-confessed snob Tina makes it perfectly clear what she thinks of his efforts

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Tina is the fourth host in Co Durham. She hopes to impress her guests with a sophisticated French menu including salmon crepes, chicken and posh peas and pear dessert. But having been highly critical of everyone else's food so far, will they now take the chance to get their own back or can she win them round?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final evening of gatherings in Co Durham sees IT manager David stage a French-themed soiree in the hope of securing the £1,000 prize. However, he soon finds his main challenge is to keep all his guests on friendly terms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Married couple Roy and Brian kick this competition off at the Plough Inn in Ripple in Kent. They're hoping their breakfast, which is 'somewhere between Little Chef and The Ritz', will have them onto a winner. However, their chances take an immediate dent when guests Simone and Anke find an old bathmat. Can a wet and wild afternoon of seal spotting on the River Stour help the hosts turn things around?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers head to Faringdon in Oxfordshire, where best friends Simone and Anke host the week's second visit at The Old Crown Coaching Inn. Roy and Brian find the furnishings fit for a king, but some of the guests find the cleanliness standards to be less-than sparkling. In the afternoon, a trip up a historic tower proves a step too far for Brian. Over a well-received breakfast the next morning, two of the guests ramp up expectations for their own visit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's third visit takes the B&Bers to Great Bromley in Essex, where married couple Richard and Colleen host at their B&B, The Courthouse. The hosts reckon they're onto a winner with their breakfast, but it's the extra touches in the rooms that really get the B&Bers singing their praises. Tensions are released during a trip to a rage room in nearby Colchester, before Richard gets teary during feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final visit is to Samsara Retreat in Kent, where Lori hosts with her friend Mike. Lori hopes to provide a relaxing, re-energising stay, and sets about giving her guests an immediate taste of what her place is all about, with a yoga, meditation and gong bath session. However, come the next morning, some of their guests aren't feeling particularly rested

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers sharpen their metaphorical knives ready for Payment Day. The Plough Inn want to get to the bottom of their cleanliness comments, before Simone and Anke try to clean up Roy and Brian's marks about their mattress. Richard from The Courthouse is brought to tears by some feedback, before Lori and Mike put their sleep scores under the spotlight. After some heated debate, the payments are revealed and the winners announced

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors are concerned that a 95-year-old woman may have suffered life-changing injuries to her hip and internal bleeding after falling down a flight of stairs, while the patient's daughter reflects on her mother's optimistic attitude to life's challenges. Elsewhere, a man knocked off his bike at 40mph may have suffered brain damage. His brother remembers a difficult time that brought them closer together

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode follows the stories of people who have lived through tragic, life-changing events, and explores how these individuals endure with the strength and support of their loved ones. An 86-year-old man is rushed to St Georges by paramedics after a suspected stroke, while an 80-year-old woman is seen by doctors after falling and sustaining an open fracture to her leg. On the paediatric ward, a mother has brought her 14-year-old daughter to see a doctor after she hurt her ankle during gymnastics

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The story of 22-year-old police officer Hazel, who is rushed to St George's after crashing her motorbike while out on a ride with her father. Meanwhile, 86-year-old John is admitted to A&E with a suspected 'triple A' - a ruptured aortic aneurysm in his abdomen, a condition that can be fatal. The doctors must work quickly to diagnose the cause of the internal bleeding, but their investigations reveal further complications. John's children share the story of their father's life, and his surprising secret that sent shockwaves through the family

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics Jas Panesar and Amy Cullen rush to a woman who has had a severe allergic reaction at a restaurant. In Birmingham, paramedics Naomi Whatley and Mark McDonagh attend to a man who has fallen unconscious after a severe diabetic attac, while in Warwick, paramedics treat a man who has collapsed with a suspected heart attack

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors are concerned that a 95-year-old woman may have suffered life-changing injuries to her hip and internal bleeding after falling down a flight of stairs, while the patient's daughter reflects on her mother's optimistic attitude to life's challenges. Elsewhere, a man knocked off his bike at 40mph may have suffered brain damage. His brother remembers a difficult time that brought them closer together

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode follows the stories of people who have lived through tragic, life-changing events, and explores how these individuals endure with the strength and support of their loved ones. An 86-year-old man is rushed to St Georges by paramedics after a suspected stroke, while an 80-year-old woman is seen by doctors after falling and sustaining an open fracture to her leg. On the paediatric ward, a mother has brought her 14-year-old daughter to see a doctor after she hurt her ankle during gymnastics

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Ending their tour of north Wales in search of the perfect pitch, the seasoned campers arrive at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey. Marine biologist-turned-campsite host Shaun welcomes the campers with freshly caught oysters and his own oyster gin, before sending them on an aquatic tour of the nearby Puffin Island. The week's winner of the coveted plaque and £2,000 cash prize is then announced

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A drunk man kicks off at the paramedics in the back of the ambulance. A taxi driver is cut out of his vehicle after a road traffic collision. Back pain leaves a landscape gardener unable to get up off the floor. A 21-year-old woman who hasn't passed urine in over 20 hours is complaining of severe pain in her kidneys, and the construction of a summer house goes badly wrong when sheets of plywood land on the builder's head

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Silas works his first ever shift in resus, and is part of the team brought in to treat a biker who is rushed to A&E after riding into a lamppost. A two-year-old boy is treated for injuries resulting from a fall, and his mother shares the story of how her eldest son was recently attacked by a gang

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the night shift in a busy A&E department. A woman with a severe form of autism is brought in, having suffered an injury to one of her fingers. While she is treated, her sister shares stories of their childhood and the challenges their family have faced. An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The campers visit Tyn-yr-Onnen farm on the edge of Snowdonia National Park, where hosts Len and Helen encourage them to embrace farm life, from feeding animals to picking mushrooms

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Lincolnshire, where they meet mother and son Sarah Jane and Josh, who are trying to restore a historic windmill, and Sandy and Andy, who plan to open a glamping site on their family farm

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet author and broadcaster Bob Flowerdew in Norfolk, where Henry eyes up a pinball machine from a damp shed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another quartet of B&B owners battle it out to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money, beginning with Paul Billington and Flora Loizou from Silverwood Lodge in Cambridgeshire. The couple get a frosty response when one guest learns her hosts do not live on-site, while another is left in hysterics by an unexpected discovery

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group sets off for Great Yarmouth to stay in Sue Dodd's dog-friendly Seashells Guesthouse. Spirits run high with a trip to the beach and a game of bingo, but Nancy's complaints over dog hairs on the sheets and smoking in the rooms bring down the light-hearted mood during dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: American ex-banker Nancy Hampson hosts at the Old Court Guesthouse in Bath, but her vocal criticisms of the week's first two establishments have made her no friends among her fellow competitors. Paul Billington is annoyed that the bed he and wife Flora Loizou are given is not big enough, but is more impressed when taken for food at a quirky local restaurant

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests stay with Caroline Scott, the owner of the Dean B&B near Longniddry, East Lothian The picturesque house and breathtaking views across the Firth of Forth win everyone over, but underwear in the drawers, cobwebs on the curtains and a messy breakfast service seriously hamper the host's chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Sue from Seashells in Great Yarmouth confronts Nancy Hampson about the controversial topic of smoking in bedrooms, while Paul Billington and Flora Loizou are held to account about their low mark for the Old Court Guesthouse

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant 49-year-old performing arts teacher Helen gets the party started in Sheffield. Her cooking doesn't go to plan, but she certainly delivers on the entertainment, on a whirlwind evening featuring trumping, spies and a dessert that goes with a bang

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sandy and Andy dream of eventually retiring to the lakes and mountains of Lazio in Italy, but for now want to use a property there to share holidays with their sizable family of five children and six grandchildren. Danni Menzies works with a budget of £70,000 to hunt out a traditional-style house with plenty of space and a big enough garden to grow fruit and vegetables

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Brannie and Paul are planning a permanent move to Portugal, and are seeking a three-bed house that offers plenty of privacy but is still walkable to amenities. With a budget of £225,000, Danni Menzies sets out to find the pair the perfect property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Eco-enthusiasts Karen and Peter from Liverpool can't wait to find their dream Spanish holiday home in the stunning region of Tarragona in Catalonia. They want a property that allows them to live off-grid, with enough space to accommodate their planned yoga retreat. Danni Menzies lines up five tempting options for their budget of £180,000, hoping that at least one will meet the couple's needs

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kent couple Alison and Lee are making a permanent move to the stunning countryside of Albox in Almería. They need a garden that's secure enough for their four dogs to roam safely, and the property must be big enough to host their six grown-up children and three grandchildren. Ben Hillman lines up some impressive properties for their budget of £150,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Julie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham, hoping to win over her guests with a no-nonsense menu she can prepare in advance. Joining her are tough-talking prison transport officer Tina, dreadlocked farm-dweller Stephen, superior IT manager David and glamorous beauty-school teacher Ashleigh. Alliances are quickly forged when David and Tina discover they both love a good gossip, before they turn their attention to Stephen when David compares him to a tramp. Julie overcooks her beef, but will her hosting make up for it and bag her the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty teacher Ashleigh throws the second of the week's parties in Co Durham, she hopes a menu of sophisticated dishes - along with a helping of hearts and love - will help her win the cash prize. However, cracks were already beginning to show in the diners' genial facade yesterday, and the hostess realises she must play peacemaker if she is to keep everyone from falling out

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Call centre complaints officer and part-time farmer Stephen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham. He hopes to win the £1,000 prize money by impressing his guests with an evening on his farm, featuring a drinks reception and rustic cuisine. However, self-confessed snob Tina makes it perfectly clear what she thinks of his efforts

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Tina is the fourth host in Co Durham. She hopes to impress her guests with a sophisticated French menu including salmon crepes, chicken and posh peas and pear dessert. But having been highly critical of everyone else's food so far, will they now take the chance to get their own back or can she win them round?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final evening of gatherings in Co Durham sees IT manager David stage a French-themed soiree in the hope of securing the £1,000 prize. However, he soon finds his main challenge is to keep all his guests on friendly terms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Married couple Roy and Brian kick this competition off at the Plough Inn in Ripple in Kent. They're hoping their breakfast, which is 'somewhere between Little Chef and The Ritz', will have them onto a winner. However, their chances take an immediate dent when guests Simone and Anke find an old bathmat. Can a wet and wild afternoon of seal spotting on the River Stour help the hosts turn things around?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers head to Faringdon in Oxfordshire, where best friends Simone and Anke host the week's second visit at The Old Crown Coaching Inn. Roy and Brian find the furnishings fit for a king, but some of the guests find the cleanliness standards to be less-than sparkling. In the afternoon, a trip up a historic tower proves a step too far for Brian. Over a well-received breakfast the next morning, two of the guests ramp up expectations for their own visit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's third visit takes the B&Bers to Great Bromley in Essex, where married couple Richard and Colleen host at their B&B, The Courthouse. The hosts reckon they're onto a winner with their breakfast, but it's the extra touches in the rooms that really get the B&Bers singing their praises. Tensions are released during a trip to a rage room in nearby Colchester, before Richard gets teary during feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final visit is to Samsara Retreat in Kent, where Lori hosts with her friend Mike. Lori hopes to provide a relaxing, re-energising stay, and sets about giving her guests an immediate taste of what her place is all about, with a yoga, meditation and gong bath session. However, come the next morning, some of their guests aren't feeling particularly rested

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers sharpen their metaphorical knives ready for Payment Day. The Plough Inn want to get to the bottom of their cleanliness comments, before Simone and Anke try to clean up Roy and Brian's marks about their mattress. Richard from The Courthouse is brought to tears by some feedback, before Lori and Mike put their sleep scores under the spotlight. After some heated debate, the payments are revealed and the winners announced

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors are concerned that a 95-year-old woman may have suffered life-changing injuries to her hip and internal bleeding after falling down a flight of stairs, while the patient's daughter reflects on her mother's optimistic attitude to life's challenges. Elsewhere, a man knocked off his bike at 40mph may have suffered brain damage. His brother remembers a difficult time that brought them closer together

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.desc: Re-enactments and archival footage uncover how the Vichy regime collaborated with the Nazis after they took over France in 1940 to help send almost 80,000 Jews from all over the country, including the 'free-zone', to concentration camps

programme.title: Ambassadors in Berlin: The Whistleblowers of WWII
programme.desc: Highlighting the actions of the diplomats in Berlin who tried to point out the dangers and reality of a Nazi Germany who went perilously ignored after Hitler was appointed chancellor in 1933

programme.title: Peleliu: WWII's Most Well-Preserved Battlefield
programme.desc: Exploring the impact of the Second World War on a small island in the Palau archipelago in Micronesia, a remote island considered the most well-preserved battlefield in the world and a living testament to one of the US Marines' most bitter and eye-opening fights in the conflict

programme.title: Village of Death: Oradour-sur-Glane 1944
programme.desc: Jeff Daniels recounts the German invasion of the French village of Oradour-sur-Glane in June 1944, during which 643 civilians were killed. He examines how the tragedy echoes through history, bearing witness to the horrors civilians endured during the Second World War

programme.title: Women of World War II: The Untold Stories
programme.desc: The stories of American women involved in the conflict, both those who served on the front lines and the home front. Featuring never-before-seen interviews with those who held the rivet guns, flew the B-17s and broke the codes. These unsung heroes inspired - and empowered - future generations by helping win the war

programme.title: Mr Polaroid
programme.desc: Before the iPhone came along, the Polaroid camera let people instantly chronicle their lives. Along with instant photo mania, its company culture became the model for Silicon Valley. This documentary tells the story of Edwin Land, co-founder of the Polaroid Corporation and the man behind the camera

programme.desc: Father Brown and Flambeau come to the aid of Christabel Carstairs, the daughter of a famous coin collector, when she is shadowed by a mysterious figure dressed in a disguise and bent on blackmail.

programme.title: The Fenn Street Gang
programme.desc: Mrs Abbott exerts her authority over Frankie one too many times, whe he goes off with his new girlfriend Celeste to the registry office.

programme.title: The Fenn Street Gang
programme.desc: Sharon has a secret. Unfortunately, it is somewhat difficult to share with Eric when they aren't even talking.

programme.title: Agony
programme.desc: Sitcom about an agony columnist on a women's magazine who advises on other people's problems but cannot solve her own. Starring Maureen Lipman and Simon Williams

programme.title: Mother's Ruin
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Roy Barraclough as a bachelor living with Dora Bryan as his belligerent mother.

programme.title: The Gaffer
programme.desc: A new opportunity with an Eastern connection tempts Fred along with new risks.

programme.title: The Gaffer
programme.desc: Fred quits and plans a new life abroad, and the workers take over, but can the factory survive?

programme.title: Doctor at Large
programme.desc: Upton must take a job in the hectic ENT unit when his rival is appointed junior house surgeon

programme.title: Second Thoughts
programme.desc: Faith is horrified to learn son Joe has been arrested for swearing at a football match, and tries to throw him out of the house. Unfortunately, she reckons without the troublesome teenager's stubborn streak, and finally finds herself forced to use more subtle means to teach him a lesson. Lynda Bellingham, James Bolam and Julia Sawalha star

programme.title: Second Thoughts
programme.desc: When Faith becomes too deeply involved in a work project for a health magazine, she turns her obsession on her family. Comedy, starring Lynda Bellingham and James Bolam

programme.title: Riders
programme.desc: First in a two-part adaptation of Jilly Cooper's racy novel about love, lust and rivalry in the ritzy world of international showjumping. Michael Praed and Marcus Gilbert star as the riders who have been bitter enemies since childhood _ and are now competing for women and the chance to become champions of the equestrian world. With Stephanie Beacham, Anthony Valentine and Caroline Harker

programme.title: Hot Girls for Men Only (1967)
programme.desc: A man leaves a trendy fashion magazine for a job at a moral reform publisher to appease his jealous fiancée, only to discover his new boss is secretly publishing an adult magazine, and their country house premises are full of glamour models. Adult comedy, starring David Kernan and Andrea Allan

programme.title: Night of the Living Dead (1968)
programme.desc: A radioactive satellite crashes on Earth, leading to the dead rising as cannibalistic zombies. A woman who witnessed her brother's death at the hands of the creatures joins a disparate group of survivors in an abandoned farmhouse - but they are soon besieged by the undead. George A Romero's horror, starring Judith O'Dea, Duane Jones, Karl Hardman and Marilyn Eastman

programme.title: We Had One of Those
programme.desc: Alex Dyke learns about another popular car from our past when he visits the National Motor Museum at Beaulieu.

programme.title: Rik Mayall Presents
programme.desc: Rik Mayall plays an aging TV show host who is convinced that he is being fired and replaced with a newer, hipper show and host. Comedy, with Peter Capaldi and William Roache.

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: Craig and Richard search for Sharron, who has disappeared while on holiday in Cornwall - and encounter spooky goings-on at an imposing manor. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: Bubblegum & Cheese
programme.desc: Alex Dyke presents an hour of bubblegum pop and cheesy tracks to make you feel good.

programme.title: Feast of Love (2007)
programme.desc: Characters from different walks of life in a small Oregon community try to resolve their romantic dilemmas, including a coffee-shop owner whose partner is tempted to leave him for her married lover, and a woman convinced by a fortune teller that her fiance does not have long to live. Drama based on the novel by Charles Baxter, with Morgan Freeman, Greg Kinnear, Radha Mitchell and Selma Blair

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Ember is forced to confess to her family that human bone fragments were found in the boat hut, but Hannah can't believe her dad was responsible and pleads with Ember to look further. When a new development propels Ember into dangerous territory, the truth is finally revealed, and it proves far more shocking than she ever expected

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A valued member of staff at the hospital is suspected of killing a vindictive media magnate who was receiving treatment. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Onboard the West Highland Line, Michael Portillo explores the stunning scenery of Scotland's west coast and islands as he heads to the Hebridean archipelago from the ferry terminal of Oban. On his way to the Isle of Mull, Michael watches eagerly for sea mammals in a stretch of water where many species of whales, dolphins and porpoises have been recorded, before checking out the daily catch in the fishing port of Tobermory. Later, Michael heads to the tiny Isle of Staffa, where he follows in the wake of a young Felix Mendelssohn, who was only 20-year-old when he set eyes on the volcanic sea cavern, Fingal's Cave

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road

programme.title: The Museum of Me
programme.desc: Six well-known Irish figures tell their stories through objects they have kept over the years. In this edition, broadcaster Bryan Dobson curates a one-night-only exhibition of personal objects, revealing the private moments behind a public life spent reporting on some of Ireland's biggest stories. Presented by Dermot Whelan

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: The western chimpanzees welcome Pia back to the troop, but how will the young female respond to her treatment after her big operation? After bidding farewell to her son Zuko, Ashanti the southern white rhinoceros gives birth to a new calf. Garth gets up close and personal with the West-African crocodiles at feeding time and Sandra joins a team from Trinity College to study biodiversity at Dublin Zoo, looking closely at the invertebrates, from hover-flies and bumblebees to butterflies and moths

programme.title: The Man Who Would Be King (1975)
programme.desc: Two British soldiers in 19th-century India travel to an unexplored land, where one of them fools the natives into believing he is a god. However, his growing delusions of grandeur put him at loggerheads with his comrade-in-arms. Adventure based on the Rudyard Kipling story, directed by John Huston, starring Sean Connery, Michael Caine, Saeed Jaffrey, Christopher Plummer and Shakira Caine.

programme.desc: James Bond achieves his '00' status and is dispatched on the trail of Le Chiffre, an international criminal financing terrorist organisations around the world. programme.title: The Name of the Rose (1986)
programme.desc: Medieval friar William of Baskerville investigates a series of murders at a remote monastery. While the monks are quick to declare the deaths the work of the Devil, William is convinced a human killer is at large, and sets out to expose the truth - a mission that brings him into conflict with the Inquisition. Mystery based on Umberto Eco's novel, starring Sean Connery, Christian Slater and F Murray Abraham.

programme.title: EuroNews
programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: Conversations with Friends
programme.desc: Frances is told the results of her abdominal scan. The diagnosis unsettles her - and she decides to keep the news to herself. Unaware of developments, Nick reveals that he and Melissa have started sleeping together again - which upsets Frances further as she reflects on how quickly things have deteriorated. The next morning Bobbi reveals that Melissa has shown her Frances's short story. Bobbi is furious with both the content of the story and Frances' dishonesty. Hurt by Frances too many times, she ends the friendship. Drama, starring Alison Oliver, Joe Alwyn, Sasha Lane and Jemima Kirke

programme.title: Shortland Street
programme.desc: Viliami uses sex to stake his claim on Jojo, but when he spots her flirtation with Logan, he lashes out and punches him

programme.title: FIFA Women's World Cup Qualifier
programme.desc: Republic of Ireland v Netherlands (Kick-off 7.30pm). Megan Campbell presents coverage of both teams' penultimate Group A2 match at Páirc Uí Chaoimh. The Dutch prevailed 2-1 in March's reverse fixture, with Lineth Beerensteyn scoring goals either side of Katie McCabe's penalty, but the Republic will hope to avenge that defeat on home soil. With analysis from Aine O'Gorman and Megan Campbell

programme.title: Challengers
programme.desc: American tennis champion Art Donaldson suffers a dip in form ahead of the 2021 US Open. To regain confidence, Art enters the Phil's Tire Town Challenger in New Rochelle and should breeze through the competition, except former doubles partner Patrick Zweig is also in the draw. Drama, starring Zendaya, Josh O'Connor and Mike Faist

programme.title: The Traitors
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final day of the game of treachery and trust. The remaining contenders have survived every banishment and murder in Claudia's castle of treachery, but it all comes down to this day. Will the Faithful weed out all the Traitors and split the prize pot between them, or will any Traitors remain undetected, and take the life-changing sum of money all for themselves?

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: A chronic gambler and bigamist starts to run out of money just as his wealthy wives start turning up dead. Police suspect that he is responsible for murder, but lack of evidence force them to rule each death a suicide. The real murderer, however, is Brown's first wife Marion, who jealously wants to keep her husband all to herself. She threatens him that if he strays again, she will commit suicide and arrange for him to be charged for her murder and those of his other wives. Short drama, starring Teresa Wright

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the career of the English actor and director, who won an Oscar for The Private Life of Henry VIII and nominated for Mutiny on the Bounty and Witness for the Prosecution

programme.title: Classic Movies: The Story Of
programme.desc: Ian Nathand examines the 1968 historical drama, which starred Peter O'Toole as Henry II, Anthony Hopkins as Richard the Lionheart and an Oscar-winning Katharine Hepburn as Eleanor of Aquitane

programme.title: Queen: Is This the Real Life?
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the extraordinary career of the British rock band, whose music has ensured a legacy that has lived on long after the death of charismatic frontman Freddie Mercury in 1991

programme.title: The Eagles: Live from the LA Forum
programme.desc: The six-time Grammy-winning rockers take to the stage at the Forum in California over three nights, delighting fans with hits including Hotel California and Take It Easy

programme.title: Isle of Wight Festival 2025
programme.desc: Coverage of the Corrs' set on the Main Stage at Seaclose Park in Newport

programme.title: Isle of Wight Festival 2025
programme.desc: Coverage of the performance by veteran Glasgow pop-rock band Texas on the Main Stage at Seaclose Park in Newport

programme.title: Billy Idol Should Be Dead
programme.desc: The musician's journey from punk rocker to pop star, featuring interviews with Idol himself, his bandmates, family and fellow artists such as Miley Cyrus and Billie Joe Armstrong

programme.title: Greatest Guitar Riffs
programme.desc: Nancy Wilson of Heart talks about the riffs that influenced her and reveals the secrets behind hits like Magic Man, Barracuda, These Dreams and What About Love?

programme.title: Women Who Rock
programme.desc: A look at female pioneers of the 1990s, when questions of feminism and control intensified and the likes of Missy Elliott, Courtney Love and Alanis Morissette made music on their own terms

programme.title: The Great Songwriters
programme.desc: Linda Perry, formerly of 4 Non Blondes, discusses the art of songwriting and performs a selection of her hits, including Beautiful and What's Up?

programme.title: Auction
programme.desc: The art collection of the singer-songwriter goes under the hammer at Christie's in a special auction featuring works by Tracey Emin, Damien Hirst and Bridget Riley

programme.title: Under Salt Marsh
programme.desc: Former detective turned teacher Jackie Ellis discovers the body of 8-year-old pupil Cefin, seemingly drowned. The discovery sends shockwaves through the community, reviving the wounds of an unsolved case that rocked the town three years prior - the disappearance of Jackie's niece Nessa, which cost her career. Crime drama, starring Kelly Reilly and Rafe Spall

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: While DJ shows off her new man at her birthday party, Deborah and Marty put their differences aside to enjoy each other's company. However, when the celebration becomes a surprise dual function, Ava is tasked with an important responsibility

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: After Marty delivers a crushing blow, Deborah and Ava travel to a comedy club in Sacramento to try out her new material, which inadvertently sparks a career-defining moment for Deborah. Meanwhile, Ava finds herself stuck between her parents' competing interests, and Marcus takes a step forward with Wilson. Comedy drama, starring Jean Smart, Hannah Einbinder and Carl Clemons-Hopkins

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: As Edison's illuminating electricity demonstration sparks conversation, George prepares for a legal battle and Bannister plots revenge

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: Bertha is reminded of her place on her visit to Newport, George gets his day in court, Peggy reveals the truth about her past and Gladys and Carrie practise for the ball

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: A woman is discovered badly beaten and left for dead and the detectives attempt to track down the culprit

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: While investigating an assault, Ross and McCoy are faced with a dilemma when they realise they have no choice but to follow the lead of their only witness, a spurned admirer of the victim. Benjamin Bratt and Carey Lowell star in the second part of a special cross-over episode that began on Law and Order

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Glasgow, one of his favourite cities, to reveal unusual and often hidden sights and sounds overlooked by the public. Starting at the Necropolis, he travels to the world's oldest music hall before enjoying the views from a city-centre landmark. He also finds the time to take tea with a film star

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to New Orleans and meets soul singer Irma Thomas at the Hotel de Monteleone, an establishment that was popular with writers such as Tennessee Williams, F Scott Fitzgerald and Ernest Hemingway. Richard speaks to Steel Magnolias author Robert Harling about the Soniat House, check into the supposedly haunted Hotel Maison de Ville and talks to burlesque dancer Chrissy Owens at the Roosevelt

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Lewis investigate the murder of a family killed in a Vietnamese restaurant and discover a moonlighting cop was also among the victims

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Bayliss are the first officers on the scene when a man is pushed between two subway cars, leaving them to uncover whether it was an accident or attempted murder. Cop drama, starring Yaphet Kotto and Kyle Secor

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: Ava tries to keep a secret from Deborah, but someone in Deborah's inner-circle leverages the truth. Meanwhile, Deborah stares down the barrel of her first radically honest show

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capital's love hotels. In the fashionable district of Meguro, Richard takes in the Claska, which was the city's first boutique hotel and has its own canine beauty salon

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the city's biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room

Later, Hank receives a quid pro quo offer

programme.title: The Fear Index
programme.desc: A scientist-turned-Wall Street tycoon becomes the target of a plot to destroy the world's financial markets. On the eve of the launch of his revolutionary AI, Dr Alex Hoffman is attacked, but not before receiving an antique book from an anonymous sender. Thriller, starring Josh Hartnett

programme.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
programme.desc: Dunk appeals to various lords in order to gain entrance into the tournament, but Egg advises him to hold on to his pride. When the Targaryens arrive at Ashford, Dunk seizes his moment with Prince Baelor

programme.desc: Part one of two. Ballard and Gharty investigate when a priest is stabbed to death in his own rectory, but allegations of sexual abuse complicate the case

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Everyday life at Northumberland's Chillingham Castle, where the members of staff try to tactfully deal with the whims of their employer, baronet Humphry Wakefield, as the estate prepares to welcome a band of ghost-hunting tourists. Meanwhile, Carlton Towers in North Yorkshire hosts an annual dog show for Afghan hounds

programme.title: The Survivor
programme.desc: Drama based on the story of Harry Haft, a survivor of the Auschwitz concentration camp who boxed his fellow inmates to stay alive. Haunted by memories and guilt, he attempts to use high-profile fights against boxing legends like Rocky Marciano as a way to find his first love again. Directed by Barry Levinson and starring Ben Foster, John Leguizamo, Peter Sarsgaard and Danny DeVito

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows (2011)
programme.desc: The detective investigates a series of seemingly unconnected crimes all around the world, and becomes convinced one man is behind them all - the elusive Professor Moriarty. He sets forth on an international journey to bring the evil mastermind to justice, but finds he is dealing with a foe who is his intellectual equal. Action thriller sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Noomi Rapace and Jared Harris

programme.title: The Last Airbender (2010)
programme.desc: A chosen one with mastery of the four elements is charged with keeping the peace between four nations. When he disappears while still a boy, one of these factions sees a chance to gain absolute power and wages war on the others. After 100 years, the chosen one is found and must find a way to restore harmony. Fantasy adventure based on the cartoon series, starring Noah Ringer and Dev Patel.

programme.title: Jerry Maguire (1996)
programme.desc: An idealistic sports agent produces a mission statement at work promising to treat clients fairly. Unfortunately, it costs him his job. Forced to set up an agency on his own, the only people prepared to stand by him are a lovelorn staff accountant and a footballer. Cameron Crowe's romantic comedy drama, with Tom Cruise, Renee Zellweger, Kelly Preston, Jonathan Lipnicki, and Cuba Gooding Jr, who won an Oscar for his performance

programme.title: About Time (2013)
programme.desc: A single man gets the chance to turn his unhappy life around when his father reveals that they both have the ability to travel through time and change their personal history. He uses his newfound talent to try to improve his love life and win the heart of an American woman he has fallen for. Romantic fantasy comedy, with Domhnall Gleeson, Rachel McAdams and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Cast Away (2000)
programme.desc: A manager for an American courier service has to fend for himself when a plane crash leaves him stranded alone on a desert island. Initially panic-stricken, he overcomes his terror to survive for four years on his own before building a raft and setting off on an epic journey back home. Adventure drama, starring Tom Hanks, Helen Hunt, Nick Searcy, Lari White and Chris Noth

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes (2009)
programme.desc: The eccentric detective and his confidant Dr Watson investigate the apparent resurrection of a criminal mastermind with links to the occult, who is carrying out a series of seemingly magical murders. The case is complicated by the reappearance of Holmes' duplicitous old flame Irene Adler. Action thriller, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Rachel McAdams, Mark Strong and Eddie Marsan

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows (2011)
programme.desc: The detective investigates a series of seemingly unconnected crimes all around the world, and becomes convinced one man is behind them all - the elusive Professor Moriarty. He sets forth on an international journey to bring the evil mastermind to justice, but finds he is dealing with a foe who is his intellectual equal. Action thriller sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Noomi Rapace and Jared Harris

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: Interstellar (2014)
programme.desc: In the future, Earth has been devastated by an environmental catastrophe and will soon become incapable of supporting human life. A former astronaut is recruited to carry out a desperate mission - piloting the last remaining spacecraft through a wormhole in search of another planet that can become humanity's new home. Sci-fi drama, starring Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway and Michael Caine

programme.title: Dunkirk (2017)
programme.desc: Allied soldiers from Belgium, the British Empire and France are surrounded by the German army, and face a tense wait for evacuation, during a fierce battle in northern France during the Second World War. Christopher Nolan's wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.title: The Natural
programme.desc: A baseball prodigy's dreams of sporting stardom are cut short when he gets shot. Once he has recovered from his injuries, he rejoins his team as a rookie recruit at an age when most players are thinking of retiring - only to enjoy an unexpected run of success. Drama, starring Robert Redford, Glenn Close, Kim Basinger, Robert Duvall and Barbara Hershey

programme.title: Lethal Weapon (1987)
programme.desc: An unstable Vietnam veteran cop teams up with a by-the-book Los Angeles police detective to investigate the apparent suicide of the daughter of a wealthy businessman, leading them to a violent and bloody confrontation with a ruthless drug cartel under the control of a retired US general. Action thriller, starring Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Gary Busey, Mitchell Ryan and Tom Atkins

programme.title: The Cinema List: Spielberg
programme.desc: This edition of the Cinema List celebrates Steven Spielberg. From the new Disclosure Day to Jurassic Park, these blockbuster adventures are top tier

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
programme.desc: An unassuming hobbit is plunged into a battle against the forces of darkness when his uncle bequeaths him a magical ring of immense power. He joins a fellowship of heroes to embark on a perilous quest to destroy the ring before it falls into the hands of its creator - a dark lord who needs it to regain his power and conquer the world. The first part of Peter Jackson's epic fantasy trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Elijah Wood, Ian McKellen, Viggo Mortensen, Sean Astin, Christopher Lee, Liv Tyler and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: Interstellar (2014)
programme.desc: In the future, Earth has been devastated by an environmental catastrophe and will soon become incapable of supporting human life. A former astronaut is recruited to carry out a desperate mission - piloting the last remaining spacecraft through a wormhole in search of another planet that can become humanity's new home. Sci-fi drama, starring Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway and Michael Caine

programme.title: Dunkirk (2017)
programme.desc: Allied soldiers from Belgium, the British Empire and France are surrounded by the German army, and face a tense wait for evacuation, during a fierce battle in northern France during the Second World War. Christopher Nolan's wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.title: Sinners (2025)
programme.desc: Hoping to escape their troubled pasts, twin brothers return to their hometown to start over, only to find an even darker force waiting to welcome them back. Writer/director Ryan Coogler's Oscar-winning horror thriller, starring Michael B Jordan, Miles Caton and Saul Williams

programme.title: One Battle After Another (2025)
programme.desc: Bob is a washed-up revolutionary who lives in a state of stoned paranoia, with his spirited and self-reliant daughter, Willa. When his evil nemesis resurfaces and Willa goes missing, he scrambles to find her as both battle the consequences of their pasts. Paul Thomas Anderson's Oscar-winning action thriller loosely based on Thomas Pynchon's novel Vineland, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, an Academy Award-winning Sean Penn, and Benicio del Toro

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Captain Phillips (2013)
programme.desc: An American cargo ship sets a dangerous course around the coast of Somalia, while inland, four men are pressed into service as pirates by the local warlords. The captain is taken hostage when the raiding party hijacks the vessel, resulting in a tense five-day crisis. Fact-based thriller, starring Tom Hanks and Barkhad Abdi

programme.title: Hannibal (2001)
programme.desc: Fugitive serial killer Dr Hannibal Lecter has gone into hiding as an academic in Italy, but a disfigured victim is determined to find him and inflict a gruesome revenge. FBI agent Clarice Starling is once again drawn into the doctor's deadly orbit as she finds herself being used as bait for the avenger's trap. Ridley Scott's thriller sequel, starring Anthony Hopkins, Julianne Moore, Gary Oldman and Ray Liotta

programme.title: True Romance (1993)
programme.desc: A shy clerk marries a hooker and kills her pimp, and together they go on the run to Los Angeles with $5million of cocaine they plan to sell. However, the drugs belong to the Mob and hitmen are soon on their trail. Tony Scott's thriller, written by Quentin Tarantino and starring Christian Slater, Patricia Arquette, Dennis Hopper, Gary Oldman, Val Kilmer, Brad Pitt and Christopher Walken

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: The Cinema List: Race Against Time
programme.desc: This edition is about the race against time. From a deadly threat in Fuze to The Desperate Hour, where Naomi Watts faces every parent's nightmare - the clock is ticking

programme.title: Tafiti - Across the Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: Dead Man's Wire (2026)
programme.desc: Tony Kiritsis is convinced mortgage broker ML Hall cheated him out of profits from a land development deal and he vows revenge. Tony walks into a branch of the Meridian Mortgage company for a meeting with ML, only to find his target is on holiday and the appointment will be with ML's son Richard instead. Regardless, Tony reveals a shotgun and holds Richard hostage for his father's supposed crimes. Director Gus Van Sant's thriller, starring Bill Skarsgård, Al Pacino and Dacre Montgomery

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: The Plague (2025)
programme.desc: A socially awkward tween endures the ruthless hierarchy at a water polo camp as his anxiety spirals into psychological turmoil over the summer. Thriller, starring Joel Edgerton and Everett Blunck

programme.title: Him
programme.desc: Cameron Cade is predicted to be the next great quarterback of American football. During a night-time training session, he suffers a traumatic brain injury. Cameron agrees to attend a week-long boot camp run by retiring star quarterback Isaiah White at his secluded desert compound. However, Cade's life soon turns into a nightmare. Supernatural horror, starring Marlon Wayans and Tyriq Withers

programme.title: One Last Deal (2026)
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent scrambles to land one last high-stakes deal for his star client, with both their futures on the line. Drama, starring Danny Dyer and Elliott Rogers

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: The Cinema List
programme.desc: It's time to kick off your Sunday shoes with remakes in this edition of the Cinema List. From Footloose to The Italian Job, revisit familiar favourites with a fresh twist

programme.title: Tafiti - Across the Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: Sisu: Road To Revenge (2025)
programme.desc: During the Second World War, Finland reluctantly cedes territory to the invading Red Army. Former Army commando Aatami Korpi is among the affected and returns to his family plot to deconstruct his timber-framed home and relocate the wooden beams on a truck across the new border separating the Soviet Union and Finland. The legend of the 'the man who refuses to die' remains a thorn in the side of the Soviet army, and a nameless KGB officer agrees to liberate high-ranking brute Igor Draganov from a Siberian prison in exchange for killing Aatami. Finnish action sequel, starring Jorma Tommila, Stephen Lang and Richard Brake

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: We Bury the Dead (2024)
programme.desc: When an experimental military weapon accidentally devastates Tasmania, volunteers travel to the island to help authorities identify the dead. Among them is a Ava Newman, who secretly hopes to find her missing husband. However, some of the bodies seem to be returning to life. Horror, starring Daisy Ridley, Brenton Thwaites and Mark Coles Smith

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A horrified family suffers as criminals smash into its business, a thief stealing clothes from charity gets a public dressing down and a foolish burglar gets stuck in a chimney. Presented by Dominic Littlewood

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A famous designer cannot believe his eyes when thieves steal his expensive spectacles, while a bicycle thief brazenly uses a power cutter in public view. Presented by Dominic Littlewood
