programme.desc: Fly-on-the-dashboard documentary showcasing outrageous road disasters, captured in video form by drivers' in-vehicle dashboard cameras

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 1997, Nicholas Burton murdered Rachel McGrath before abducting a student. After a complex trial, the killer was handed three life sentences

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: The murder of 17-year-old Terry Edmonds, last seen getting off a train in Tunbridge Wells in 2006, and the painstaking forensic evidence used to unmask the teenager's killer

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: Police use forensic evidence to catch the perpetrator who murdered a mother in her home in 1994

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of the serial killer Todd Christopher Kohlhepp, who claimed the lives of at least seven innocent people between 2003 and 2016

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.sub-title: Malone: The Killer with No Conscience
programme.desc: The story of Garry Malone, who bludgeoned his wife to death before making a bogus TV appeal for her to come home

programme.title: Murdered at First Sight
programme.desc: As 13-year-old Lauren Landavoza walks home from school, a 22-year-old loner is getting ready to ensure that if he can't have her, no one can

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: The killing of Vikki Thompson, bludgeoned to death in Oxfordshire - and how the hunt to bring her killer to justice would take more than 15 years

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look at the life and crimes of American serial killer Charles Cullen who, over his 16-year career as a nurse, admits to killing as many as 40 people and is currently serving 18 consecutive life sentences in prison. He will not be eligible for parole until 2403

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 21-year-old Anthony Arkwright from South Yorkshire, who in 1988 went on a killing spree in which he murdered three people - including his own grandfather

programme.title: Murder Next Door
programme.desc: A close-knit community rocked by the disappearance of a Scottish teenager in March 2016. As the hours passed without trace, friends, family and neighbours began to fear the worst for the fate of Paige Doherty

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: How the investigation into the murder of Philip Inhofer at his home in Sacramento led police to a 20-year-old call girl known as Bat Girl

programme.title: The Real CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Tragedy struck the University of Miami when linebacker Marlin Barnes and his girlfriend were found murdered in his apartment. With no witnesses, investigators delve into Miami's hip-hop scene to unravel the mystery behind their deaths

programme.title: Killing Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Crime documentary investigating cases of couples murdered by their own children. The first episode focuses on Stephen Seddon, who tried to kill his parents in a fake crash, but was then hailed as a hero for saving them. But his evil intentions hadn't got him anywhere

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.sub-title: Dayton Leroy Rogers - The Molalla Forest Killer
programme.desc: A profile of one of Oregon's most prolific killers, Dayton Leroy Rogers, who would prowl Molalla's forests in search of prostitutes to satisfy his lust for violence

programme.title: Murder Uncut
programme.desc: After hosting a barbecue, two people were shot dead in their driveway - but the identity of the killer was even more shocking

programme.title: Murders That Shocked the Nation
programme.desc: The case of the A6 killer James Hanratty, who was hanged in 1962 as one of the last people in England to be executed before the death penalty was abolished. His brother Michael, Geoffrey Bindman QC and witness John Kerr are all interviewed by Fred Dinenage and offer their views on the controversial guilty verdict

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A master manipulator is accused of the murder of his wife, and is eventually caught out by a polygraph test - and his own fiancee

programme.title: Border Security USA
programme.desc: Puerto Rican police join forces with the American coastguard to pursue two men transporting suspicious bundles across open seas

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: A couple are lucky to be alive after a collision involving a truck. Police catch two dedicated marijuana enthusiasts, and a woman blatantly breaks the law in front of the cops

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: A speeding driver gets more than he bargains for, and a van and car collide at high speed. Plus, an incoherent drunk man heads off to another city on foot

programme.title: Stop, Search, Seize
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Ireland's border force officers as they fight to stop gangs of organised smugglers from bringing drugs, money, guns, cars and even exotic animals into the country

programme.title: UK Border Force

programme.desc: Elite officers Rosie and Darren carry out a manhunt through the gardens of an upmarket estate, while Chris and Simon come face to face with a notorious drug addict in Milton Keynes

programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood hosts the return of the show looking at innovative methods used to catch criminals. This time, a Good Samaritan is revealed to be a violent offender

programme.desc: In 1996, two girls and their mother enjoying a walk in the Kent countryside were the victims of a frenzied hammer attack by Michael Stone, who left them for dead. Lin Russell and her six-year-old daughter Megan died from their injuries, but nine-year-old Josie survived and made a remarkable recovery. This film examines the events surrounding the murders and features interviews with Shaun Russell - Lin's widower and the girls' father - Josie's close friend Lucy Gregson, her former primary school teacher Lynda Roberts and police family liaison officer Pauline Smith

programme.title: Apocalypse D-Day
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the key turning point of the Second World War, which started on June 6, 1944. A titanic fleet took on the beaches of Normandy, intent on liberating Europe from Hitler's Nazis. Thanks to the lessons learned from the Dieppe raid of August 1942 and supported by the Provence landing of August 1944, the mission was a spectacular success as the Allies succeeded in moving beyond their political differences, overcoming logistical difficulties and carrying out an operation with unprecedented military stakes

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.desc: Drama-documentary using the Colosseum to tell the thrilling story of the rise and fall of the Roman Empire, through the lens of the men and women who fought and died there. In AD80, the Colosseum is open for business. Roman emperor Titus plans 100 days of games to commemorate, including an epic battle to the death between gladiators Priscus and Verus

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: On July 1, 2000, eight-year-old Sarah Payne went missing while playing in a field near her grandparents' home in West Sussex, and her disappearance prompted a nationwide search. On July 17, her body was found near Pulborough, and soon after convicted paedophile Roy Whiting was charged with her abduction and murder. In this documentary, Sara Payne relives the day her daughter went missing and recounts the horror and agonising days that unfolded in its wake. She also speaks about her fight to change the system in the UK so parents could find out if a child sex offender was living in their area

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: An analysis of ancient monuments, questioning what compelled man to move colossal stones long distances to assemble remarkably similar configurations in several locations around the world. Some people believe that extraterrestrials instructed these complex structures be built. Presented by Giorgio Tsoukalos

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: On July 1, 2000, eight-year-old Sarah Payne went missing while playing in a field near her grandparents' home in West Sussex, and her disappearance prompted a nationwide search. On July 17, her body was found near Pulborough, and soon after convicted paedophile Roy Whiting was charged with her abduction and murder. In this documentary, Sara Payne relives the day her daughter went missing and recounts the horror and agonising days that unfolded in its wake. She also speaks about her fight to change the system in the UK so parents could find out if a child sex offender was living in their area

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: Treblinka, Majdanek and Sobibor, the camps used in Operation Reinhardt, a murder campaign in which the Nazis murdered almost two million people over two months in 1942 and 1943

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The crucial role ships have played in international conflict over the past 500 years, beginning with the battleship's declining influence during the 20th century

programme.title: Instruments of Death
programme.desc: Karl Ude-Martinez investigates weapons used during 1916's Battle of the Somme, including machine guns and poison gas

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: While more than 1.5 billion people believe in reincarnation, ancient astronaut theorists suggest that extraterrestrials designed the human body to be a vessel for otherworldly entities

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: American Pickers

They then pay a visit to Sammy?s classic car paradise - a dream-come-true for car enthusiasts and pickers alike

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones heads to Gwynedd to explore the history of Caernarfon Castle, the building of which began in 1283 on the orders of Edward I. He demonstrates how the castle's vast stone walls and strategically designed towers repelled all enemies, even the Welsh national hero Owain Glyndwr, earning itself a truly forbidding reputation, but its military importance faded at the end of the 15th century when Henry Tudor - a Welshman - made his way on to the English throne

programme.title: Hells Angels: Kingdom Come
programme.desc: The twisted relationship between Greg 'Picasso? Woolley and Marty Robert, two of the most powerful Hells Angels in history, and how it came to a fatal head

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists suggest that some extraterrestrials may closely resemble animals, as evidenced by gods depicted with animal traits. Researchers have have contemplated that some animals may have been planted on Earth by aliens as part of an experiment to shape life on Earth

programme.title: Apocalypse D-Day
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of the key turning point of the Second World War, which started on June 6, 1944. A titanic fleet took on the beaches of Normandy, intent on liberating Europe from Hitler's Nazis. Thanks to the lessons learned from the Dieppe raid of August 1942 and supported by the Provence landing of August 1944, the mission was a spectacular success as the Allies succeeded in moving beyond their political differences, overcoming logistical difficulties and carrying out an operation with unprecedented military stakes

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: The military helicopter Bell UH-1 series was designed and built by the Bell Helicopter Company in the late 1950s as a multipurpose machine. It later became synonymous with the Vietnam War, being used to transport troops and equipment as well as for air-assault missions

programme.title: Kevin Costner's The West
programme.desc: An epic journey through the birth of the American West, examining the relentless competition for land, power, and identity that forged a nation's history, spanning a century of pivotal events. The host reflects on late 18th-century war leader Chief Little Turtle's battle to resist American expansion into the Northwest Territory. This represented a struggle for sovereignty and survival for the Native American tribes during a time of great change. The stakes were high as Chief Little Turtle rallied his people, showcasing their bravery and determination

programme.title: Kevin Costner's The West
programme.desc: Hunter and explorer John Colter, who was one of the first Americans to explore the West. He later entered the booming fur trade. When he repeatedly ignored warnings from the Blackfeet Indians tribe about hunting sacred beaver on their land, he was taken captive and forced to make a run for his life

programme.title: The Curse of Oak Island
programme.desc: New revelations are made about the sand and stone road in the swamp, as a discovery in the Money Pit suggests that answers to the mystery may be found over 2000 miles away

programme.sub-title: The Return of the Return of San Burrito

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: Despite the Germans effort to protect France, the Allies' invasion ended in triumphant liberation. Here, some of the brave soldiers involved reveal their first-hand stories

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A chronological appraisal of the dramatic D-Day story, focusing on the vessels that made the landings on the beaches of Normandy possible

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days to Beat the Third Reich
programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. It was a period that made history, both heroic and tragic. The first of two programmes retraces events, day by day, from D-Day up to the Liberation of Paris, through a series of moving testimonies and spectacular colourised archives

programme.title: D-Day: 100 Days to Beat the Third Reich

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the country to discover what life was like during the Bronze and Iron ages before the Romans invaded. In Norfolk, she helps to uncover a prehistoric monument that has been hidden for 2,000 years, and examines the Hebridean skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies, learning each is made up of parts from different people

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the country to learn more about mankind's Stone Age ancestors, and travels to Jersey to meet a team hoping to shed new light on the Neanderthals. In the Preseli Hills in Pembrokeshire, she learns about an investigation into the mystery surrounding Stonehenge, and hears stories of cannibalism and the ritual use of human skulls during the Ice Age

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part documentary, the naturalist explores the origins of life on Earth. Beginning the journey near his childhood home in Charnwood Forest, Leicestershire, he learns how a fossil discovery transformed scientists' understanding of the evolution of complex organisms, and in Newfoundland and Australia he reveals the earliest kinds of animal to exist on the planet. With the help of cutting-edge technology and visual effects, the programme brings the creatures to life, demonstrating how they survived

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary, the naturalist travels to Burgess Shale in Canada's Rocky Mountains, where he explores how evolution gained pace once creatures developed mouths and mobility. In Scotland, he reveals that one of the smallest fossils found holds the secret to how animals began surviving on dry land, and explains the bloom of life during the oxygen-rich Carboniferous period

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural History Museum Alive

He relates how his respect for the sanctity of human life clashed with his blood-lust, and how the pair came to be adoptive parents to a young girl. Gothic horror based on the novel by Anne Rice, with Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, Kirsten Dunst, Antonio Banderas and Christian Slater

It's the beginning of a friendship which culminates in an epic showdown as Elphaba transforms a renegade witch with a skill for bursting into show-stopping songs, like Defying Gravity. Part one of The Wizard of Oz prequel based on the hit stage musical, starring Cynthia Erivo, Ariana Grande-Butera, Jonathan Bailey, Michelle Yeoh and Jeff Goldblum

He takes piano lessons, makes his way into the Royal Academy of Music, and hones his craft in local pubs before becoming one of the world's biggest pop stars. Golden Globe-winning biopic, starring Taron Egerton, Jamie Bell and Richard Madden

programme.title: Am I OK? (2022)
programme.desc: Lifelong pals Lucy and Jane face a test of friendship when one of them embarks on a personal journey. Romantic comedy drama, starring Dakota Johnson, Sonoya Mizuno and Jermaine Fowler

Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Kate Winslet, Ian McKellen, Shane Richie, Jean Reno and Bill Nighy

Animated adventure, with the voices of Lacey Chabert, Tim Curry, Tom Kane, Cree Summer, Lynn Redgrave and Jodi Carlisle

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets (2016)
programme.desc: When rivalry leaves them cast adrift on the streets of New York, two bickering hounds must put their differences aside and pull together. Animated adventure from the makers of Despicable Me, featuring the voices of Louis CK, Eric Stonestreet and Kevin Hart

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets 2 (2019)
programme.desc: Max the terrier encounters canine-intolerant cows, hostile foxes and a scary turkey when he visits the countryside. Luckily for the lovable dog, he soon catches a break when he meets Rooster, a gruff farm dog who tries to cure the pooch of his neuroses. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Patton Oswalt, Kevin Hart and Harrison Ford

programme.desc: When a gang of animal crooks set out on what should be a simple con, they're swept into a train robbery. It falls to Falcon, a small-time raccoon thief, and Rex, a by-the-book police dog, to put their differences aside and protect the passengers aboard a runaway high-speed train. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Wyatt Bowen and Daniel Brochu

programme.title: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs (2009)

When the device gets launched into the atmosphere, it has a strange effect on his town's weather, causing giant meals to fall from the sky to the delight of the residents - until it gets out of control. Animated comedy, with the voices of Bill Hader, Anna Faris and James Caan

programme.title: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs 2 (2013)

Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Bill Hader, Anna Faris and James Caan

programme.desc: John Denver was one of the most successful and best-oved recording artists of the 20th century. programme.title: Cúl Stáitse - Oireachtas 2025
programme.desc: Since it first began over 125 years ago the Oireachtas festival has hosted some of the most prestigious competitions in traditional arts and has grown from an event frequented by the hundreds to now been one of the most important cultural events in Ireland. This year's festival in Belfast had over 1,000 competitors taking to the stage in song, music, dancing and storytelling competitions. This documentary follows the competitors, some taking to the stage for the first time, others returning veterans, while all strive for perfection and a place in the history books

programme.title: Pobal na hEaragaile

programme.title: Carna an Cheoil agus na nAmhrán
programme.desc: For over a century, music collectors have flocked to Carna in Connemara, drawn by its rich Gaelic traditions. Here, songs, music, dance, and storytelling have been preserved, offering a rare glimpse into Ireland's ancient cultural roots. The echoes of those early collectors still linger in Carna today, and their recordings continue to shape its cultural identity. This programme examines Carna's vibrant heritage, exploring the treasures passed down through generations and meeting the new wave of locals proudly keeping their native culture alive today

programme.title: Croí na Féile

programme.desc: Teenagers Dean and Cindy meet and instantly fall in love, marrying soon after. Years later, they have a child but their relationship is floundering as resentments have built up between them. They spend a night together in a hotel room, making a final attempt to revive their failing marriage. Drama, starring Ryan Gosling and Michelle Williams

programme.title: Furia
programme.sub-title: Lose Yourself and All Is Lost
programme.desc: A policeman moves with his daughter to a small, picturesque town in western Norway. However, there are darker forces lurking under the idyllic surface and soon Asgeir is mixed up in something much bigger and more dangerous than just small-town crime. Events lead him to cross paths with the mysterious woman, Ragna. Drama, starring Ine Marie Wilmann, Pål Sverre Hagen and Preben Hodneland. In Norwegian and German

programme.title: Transatlantic Sessions - Togha agus Rogha
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh present highlights from two decades of the legendary music show, featuring musical stars from Ireland, the UK and the US, including Rosanne Cash, Mary Chapin Carpenter and Teddy Thompson. Fans of Transatlantic Sessions can see Sarah Jarosz and Alison Krauss sing Run Away and Sharon Shannon and Jim Murray give an uplifting performance of Dreamcatcher - Off the Hook. Karen Matheson is joined by Cara Dillon to close the programme with a rousing performance of The Diamond Ring

programme.title: Cúramóirí
programme.desc: Documentary delving into the heart of daily Irish life, illustrating the crucial work and life saving care provided by family carers, professional carers and dedicated organisations to those who need it the most. Dublin's Diarmuid Mac Dubhglais provides food for those who are hungry,Pádraig Ó hAiniféin from Corca Dhuibhne tells of his spinal cord injury and palliative care nurse at the Galway Hospice Úna O'Kane demonstrates her work

programme.title: Hector Caribbean
programme.desc: Hector Ó hEochagáin gets to grips with the multicultural influences of life in the Dominican Republic, starting with a visit to a local baseball club, a cornerstone of Dominican life. Next, he experiences the unique rhythms of 'son', a distinctive musical genre of the island, and he even shows off a few moves. Hector tours a plantain farm where he learns how this staple of Caribbean cuisine is grown, before visiting the border with Haiti, where he watches as hundreds of people cross the bridge between the two countries to engage in trade

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.desc: Dorien splashes out on a trip to the Canary Islands, but with Tracey's fear of flying, Sharon's hopes for adventure and the novelist's own eagerness for anything in trousers, will it be plain sailing? Linda Robson, Pauline Quirke and Lesley Joseph star in this 2014 Christmas special

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.sub-title: There's a Girl in My Souk
programme.desc: Travis has finally been allowed to fly the nest on a gap year to Spain, but nobody has heard from him in four days and he was last seen getting on a ferry to Morocco with his girlfriend. Tracey is convinced something terrible has happened to him so she heads off to Tangier to track him down and bring him back home for Christmas, accompanied by Sharon and Dorien. On arrival they end up in a bar run by Vince, one of Dorien's old flames, who reviews the CCTV footage and spots Travis being arrested in a police raid. Festive special of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with Martin Kemp

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.sub-title: The House for the Rising Sons
programme.desc: The festive season is around the corner in Chigwell and Tracey has got big plans that could upset the harmony in the house over the holiday period. Meanwhile, Sharon has come up with a novel approach to doing her Christmas shopping and Dorien has got a mystery to solve. What could possibly go wrong? Laurence Marks and Maurice Gran's long-running comedy returns for a festive special, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph as the three friends and sparring partners, with Emily Atack

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.sub-title: We Gotta Get Out of This Place

Dorien is now ruling the roost, having bought the house, and she and Tracey have been getting on each other's nerves. To make matters worse, Tracey is missing sister Sharon, who is stuck on a cruise ship in Costa Rica, and Dorien is up to her old tricks with a rich dentist. Does he know what he's getting himself into? Return of the comedy, starring Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with Les Dennis and Ami Metcalf

Laetitia Maklouf decorates a tiny balcony, and Charlotte Uhlenbroek shares the secrets of the perfect coastal wildlife garden

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits Wendover in Buckinghamshire to show how to create a rose garden, and comes up with ideas for displaying the plants, including rustic pergolas. Laetitia Maklouf demonstrates the making of rose water, and Valentine Warner shows how the rose can be used as an ingredient for a spicy sauce

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Londoners Lily and Natasha are in their mid-twenties and keen to get onto the property ladder. Currently renting a one-bed flat in east London, they have impulsively decided to buy a 35-year-old narrowboat at a knock-down price that needs considerable work. They are determined to save money and do all the work themselves, but it's a significant challenge as neither has any experience of fixing up - or living on - a boat, let alone any knowledge of plumbing, electrics, fabrication work or anything renovation-relevant

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee Burkhill and Flo Headlam are in Rocester, Staffordshire to help Julie and Phillip create a wildlife-focused space in their back garden where they can spend the small amount of time they get together in peace. With a budget of just £3,000, Lee and Flo have to get creative

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Charlton Hayes, South Gloucestershire, Alisha and Toby ask Chris Hull and Flo Headlam to help transform their sloping garden that has nowhere for them or their pet rabbits to enjoy

programme.title: Au Pair Girls (1972)
programme.desc: A group of foreign girls travel to England to work as au pairs, but they are easily distracted from their new jobs by the men they meet. Comedy, starring Gabrielle Drake, Astrid Frank and Johnny Briggs

programme.title: Cruel Passion (1977)
programme.desc: Two sisters are expelled from a nunnery and are plunged into a life of debauchery. While one embraces her new lifestyle, the other is desperate to cling to her old morality and sees a hope of salvation in a mysterious aristocrat who harbours a hidden dark side. Historical drama based on the novel Justine by the Marquis de Sade, starring Koo Stark and Martin Potter

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits East Wittering in Sussex, where he takes a tour of a seaside garden. He reveals how best to adapt plots to cope with damaging sea winds, and demonstrates how to make a nautical-themed obelisk. Laetitia Maklouf decorates a tiny balcony, and Charlotte Uhlenbroek shares the secrets of the perfect coastal wildlife garden

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits Wendover in Buckinghamshire to show how to create a rose garden, and comes up with ideas for displaying the plants, including rustic pergolas. Laetitia Maklouf demonstrates the making of rose water, and Valentine Warner shows how the rose can be used as an ingredient for a spicy sauce

programme.title: The Greatest Auction
programme.desc: AJ Odudu hosts as millionaires, private collectors and antique dealers compete to buy extraordinary items. Banksy experts take a risk on a piece of wall featuring work attributed to the anonymous artist and Tommy Cooper's iconic fez goes on sale. Plus, a jar of pickled pig foetuses and an array of Buzzcocks memorabilia

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: In Derbyshire, antiques expert Caroline Hawley, trader Danny Sebastian and aeronautical engineer come upcycler Stu Abbott head to Buxton where they go head to head as they rummage through its antique shops, scrap yards and flea markets in the hope of finding any items that they can each turn into a healthy profit

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping programming bringing you a selection of innovative products

programme.title: Cruise TV with LoveitBookit
programme.desc: Teleshopping feature bringing you the hottest destinations and latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill head to Cheadle Hulme, Greater Manchester, to transform Adyan and Araam's garden, which currently features are a long strip of concrete leading to nowhere and a six-inch-high barbecue. They add a pergola that incorporates a swing, a revised barbecue area and attractive flower and produce beds

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull head to Godalming, Surrey, to transform a new plot on a budget of £6,000. Chloe and Peter's garden has a strict tree preservation order on four of the trees. The couple envision a blend of arts and crafts and French formality in their new garden. The two designers set about interpreting these very different, almost conflicting ideas in their pitches before the successful proposal is chosen and acted upon

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits East Wittering in Sussex, where he takes a tour of a seaside garden. He reveals how best to adapt plots to cope with damaging sea winds, and demonstrates how to make a nautical-themed obelisk. Laetitia Maklouf decorates a tiny balcony, and Charlotte Uhlenbroek shares the secrets of the perfect coastal wildlife garden

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits Wendover in Buckinghamshire to show how to create a rose garden, and comes up with ideas for displaying the plants, including rustic pergolas. Laetitia Maklouf demonstrates the making of rose water, and Valentine Warner shows how the rose can be used as an ingredient for a spicy sauce

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of locals living along Yorkshire's coastline. This episode features members of the Robson family from Bridlington, who've been fishing the North Sea for generations, as they head up the coast to Whitby for a boat repair. Volunteers at Bempton Cliffs work to protect and preserve almost half a million seabirds, and a group of sea swimmers take a full-moon sea swim at Scarborough

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Whitby distillers Jess and Luke are creating their latest tipple from tasty foraged seaside ingredients. In Scarborough, pioneering sea-farmer Wave checks out his seaweed harvest of sugar kelp, while newbie hoteliers Steph and Luke prepare for the busiest weekend of the summer season. Down the coast in Filey, military vehicle restorer Duncan preps his Second World War American Jeep for parade duty

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Fisherman Darren heads into the North Sea from Staithes in search of lobster and crab. Shellfish are now a mainstay of the catch on the Yorkshire coast, with conservationists ensuring that the lobster population remains buoyant by breeding and releasing them from a purpose-built hatchery in Whitby. Plus, a new recruit joins the team running the historic tramway in Saltburn, and an ambitious couple launch the first wine grown in their Robin Hood's Bay vineyard

programme.title: Futtock's End (1970)
programme.desc: A general invites a motley group of guests to stay at his country estate - and chaos ensues. Silent comedy. starring Ronnie Barker, Michael Hordern and Roger Livesey

programme.title: Biggles: Adventure in Time (1986)
programme.desc: A New York businessman is mysteriously transported back in time to the First World War, where he meets the famed British flying ace Biggles. The time traveller discovers he has been sent back to help the daredevil pilot destroy a German secret weapon that could change the course of history. Sci-fi adventure, starring Neil Dickson, Alex Hyde-White and Peter Cushing in his final film role

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill head to Cheadle Hulme, Greater Manchester, to transform Adyan and Araam's garden, which currently features are a long strip of concrete leading to nowhere and a six-inch-high barbecue. They add a pergola that incorporates a swing, a revised barbecue area and attractive flower and produce beds

programme.title: Garden Rescue

The two designers set about interpreting these very different, almost conflicting ideas in their pitches before the successful proposal is chosen and acted upon

programme.title: Mary Millington: Confessions from the David Galaxy Affair (1979)
programme.desc: A seductive astrologer's carefree life is brought crashing to a halt when he is accused of robbery and cannot win over the only person able to provide an alibi. He faces a possibly greater challenge in providing a woman who has never had an orgasm with the satisfaction she has long been deprived of. Sex comedy, starring Alan Lake and Diana Dors

programme.title: Groupie Girl (1970)
programme.desc: Sally runs away from her strict upbringing to become a rock band's groupie. A cautionary tale of hairy hippies and heartbreak. Drama starring Esme Johns and Donald Sumpter.

programme.title: Can You Keep it Up for a Week? (1974)
programme.desc: A workshy man's fiancee challenges him to hold down a steady job - a situation that puts him squarely in the path of temptation. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Jeremy Bulloch, Neil Hallett and Jill Damas

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Roo Irvine, market trader Gary Barton and blacksmith upcycler Kevin Paxton head to St Andrews to find some bargains they can turn into profit for Children in Need

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's UK Killer Evidence
programme.desc: The story of what happened when a drug dealer was shot in Southampton. With very little forensic evidence to go on, police had to rely on the description of a witness who got away.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Blackpool Double Murder
programme.desc: Geoffrey Wansell explores two eerily similar murders that take place ten years apart in Blackpool when two elderly women are killed.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: A Random Murder
programme.desc: A look into the case of Kevin Bennett, who was murdered in 2014 by Connor and Brandon Doran and friend Simon Evans, after they attacked him as he slept rough.

programme.title: Britain's Countryside Killers
programme.desc: The story of Lindsay Birbeck, who, in August 2019, was murdered by 16-year-old Rocky Marciano Price after he targeted her during a walk.

programme.title: Britain's Almost Perfect Murders

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Parachute Murder Plot

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's UK Killer Evidence

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives

programme.title: Fatal Fall: Murder or Accident?

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Murder proves to be no laughing matter when Father Brown visits a jesters' convention. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams as the sleuthing priest, with Neil Pearson and Paul Copley

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: After a journalist is killed searching for buried pirate treasure, Charlie and Rex dig deep into the competitive world of treasure-hunters to unearth the killer

programme.desc: The investigation into a brutal murder in a public toilet has Murdoch butting heads with Inspector Choi as he closes in on the killer

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective discovers a world of bribery and scandal when he investigates the case of a beauty queen electrocuted during a demonstration to illustrate the dangers of alternating current electricity. Drama, set in 19th-century Canada, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the death of a well-known Toronto lawyer, after his body is delivered in a trunk with a threatening note addressed to Inspector Brackenreid. Meanwhile, Murdoch's analytical approach to his work catches the eye of the chief constable who suggests to him that he may be the ideal candidate for a vacant position at a higher level. Drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective tries to prove the innocence of a woman suspected of murdering her husband, a well known boxer. Despite being found standing over his dead body holding a gun, Murdoch believes the blood splatter evidence suggests someone else is to blame. Drama, starring Yannick Bisson

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Murder proves to be no laughing matter when Father Brown visits a jesters' convention. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams as the sleuthing priest, with Neil Pearson and Paul Copley

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: Jane believes a doctor's murderer was a fellow physician, and discovers the victim was the head of a transplant unit. He is convinced wealthy people are being given preferential treatment at the hospital where the medic worked, but his sleuthing lands him in trouble with a patient's gun-wielding bodyguard. Elsewhere, Lisbon is demoted following a slip of the tongue. Crime drama, starring Simon Baker

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: While Sarah embarks on a wilderness adventure, Charlie and Rex investigate the murder of a college professor

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: Promoted alongside Al to Manhattan's Major Crimes unit following a series of impressive arrests, Carrie is now required to use her gift in different ways. An investigation into the kidnapping of an 11-year-old girl cuts close to home in its similarities to Rachel's death. Starring Poppy Montgomery and Dylan Walsh, with Dallas Roberts (The Good Wife, The Walking Dead) joining the cast as Carrie's whip-cracking new boss

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The second of a two-part story picks up where things left off last week, with Humphrey, Florence and Dwayne having swapped the sun and sand of the Caribbean for the cold and noise of London. Their latest investigation has taken them back to the detective inspector's home town - but they've hit a brick wall with the discovery of another dead body. And now they have two murders to solve. Meanwhile, Dwayne has an emotional encounter and Humphrey receives advice from an unexpected source that helps him reach a big decision. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall, Danny John-Jules and Josephine Jobert, with guest appearances from Ardal O'Hanlon and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The clerical sleuth encounters a voodoo priest who is intent on winning back the woman he loves - no matter the cost. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part One
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, a fishing community is excited about the arrival of their festival queen for an annual celebration, but when her boat arrives with only her dead body on board, DI Mooney is left, yet again, with a baffling mystery to solve. What could a missing loaf of bread have to do with the crime? Meanwhile, Florence and Patrice look forward to their wedding, which is only a week away, but a huge strain is put on their relationship when the groom is drawn into the murder case as a former employer and long-time friend of the victim

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team are left stunned by another murder connected to the killing of festival queen Tiana. But there is a new lead - a phone call relating to their initial suspect - so DI Jack and his officers travel over to nearby Rosalie Island, where the owner and employees of a lucrative investment company all fall under suspicion as they are questioned about the mysterious call. Meanwhile, with emotions running high, Ruby encourages her uncle, Commissioner Patterson, to show his emotional side for once. Guest starring Angus Deayton and Saskia Reeves

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Murder proves to be no laughing matter when Father Brown visits a jesters' convention. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams as the sleuthing priest, with Neil Pearson and Paul Copley

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: Jane believes a doctor's murderer was a fellow physician, and discovers the victim was the head of a transplant unit. He is convinced wealthy people are being given preferential treatment at the hospital where the medic worked, but his sleuthing lands him in trouble with a patient's gun-wielding bodyguard. Elsewhere, Lisbon is demoted following a slip of the tongue. Crime drama, starring Simon Baker

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: While Sarah embarks on a wilderness adventure, Charlie and Rex investigate the murder of a college professor

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: Promoted alongside Al to Manhattan's Major Crimes unit following a series of impressive arrests, Carrie is now required to use her gift in different ways. An investigation into the kidnapping of an 11-year-old girl cuts close to home in its similarities to Rachel's death. Starring Poppy Montgomery and Dylan Walsh, with Dallas Roberts (The Good Wife, The Walking Dead) joining the cast as Carrie's whip-cracking new boss

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The second of a two-part story picks up where things left off last week, with Humphrey, Florence and Dwayne having swapped the sun and sand of the Caribbean for the cold and noise of London. Their latest investigation has taken them back to the detective inspector's home town - but they've hit a brick wall with the discovery of another dead body. And now they have two murders to solve. Meanwhile, Dwayne has an emotional encounter and Humphrey receives advice from an unexpected source that helps him reach a big decision. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall, Danny John-Jules and Josephine Jobert, with guest appearances from Ardal O'Hanlon and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part One
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, a fishing community is excited about the arrival of their festival queen for an annual celebration, but when her boat arrives with only her dead body on board, DI Mooney is left, yet again, with a baffling mystery to solve. What could a missing loaf of bread have to do with the crime? Meanwhile, Florence and Patrice look forward to their wedding, which is only a week away, but a huge strain is put on their relationship when the groom is drawn into the murder case as a former employer and long-time friend of the victim

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: Beyond the Shining Sea - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team are left stunned by another murder connected to the killing of festival queen Tiana. But there is a new lead - a phone call relating to their initial suspect - so DI Jack and his officers travel over to nearby Rosalie Island, where the owner and employees of a lucrative investment company all fall under suspicion as they are questioned about the mysterious call. Meanwhile, with emotions running high, Ruby encourages her uncle, Commissioner Patterson, to show his emotional side for once. Guest starring Angus Deayton and Saskia Reeves

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Suspicions are raised when a routine x-ray reveals suspicious packages hidden inside the tyres of a pick-up truck. Officers also seize a large shipment of marijuana

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Bologna in northern Italy, a city so famous for its food that it's known as the stomach of Italy. He learns how to make the local fresh egg pasta used for tortellini, tagliatelle, ravioli and lasagne and enjoys stuffed rabbit, filled with Parma ham and Parmesan frittata for dinner. Later, Rick drives up into the hills to a cheese dairy where he tastes a 13-year aged Parmesan and enjoys lunch in at a pig farm with restaurant attached, where huge charcoal-grilled pork steaks are served

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Palermo in northern Sicily, a city echoing Arab, North African, French, Spanish and Italian influences, making its cuisine unique and irresistible. Pine nuts, raisins, almonds and golden breadcrumbs are combined with flavours of sun-ripened tomatoes, lemons and the finest olive oil to create a range of unforgettable dishes. Rick witnesses locals celebrating a festival in honour of Palermo's patron saint Rosalia, and receives a cookery lesson from a real-life duchess. At home he cooks Marsala chicken, a Sicilian classic served in UK bistros since the 1960s

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond tests the Smart car, which is fitted with dual engines and turbos, James May considers the rejuvenation of Birmingham and Jaguar's XJ6, and Jeremy Clarkson compares two Type R Honda cars with the Civic and NSX. Carol Vorderman is this week's Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and, with the Olympic Games just around the corner, the team invent a long-jump competition for vehicles

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine, featuring a race to Monte Carlo between Jeremy Clarkson in an Aston Martin DB9, and Richard Hammond and James May, who opt to take a high-speed train. Actress Fay Ripley is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, while a nun swaps a Vauxhall Nova for a monster truck

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: The pair fish at Christchurch's Bridge Pool in Dorset, hoping to catch sea trout, before Paul persuades Bob to charter a boat in search of sea bass. They venture out to the Needles where Paul makes a incredible catch, and decide to take the fish to a local restaurant to prepare it

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon explores the eastern Mediterranean, beginning in Cyprus, where he goes on patrol with UN peacekeepers overseeing an abandoned buffer zone. He moves on to Lebanon, where he discovers the roots of Christianity, and Israel, where he boards a navy vessel guarding the country's offshore oil reserves. Finally, he crosses the world's most heavily fortified borders into Gaza, where he meets a woman helping reconstruction efforts by making bricks into ash

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore more of the nation's dishes, beginning by showing there is more to British fowl than roast chicken. They prepare duck tikka and a one-pot partridge recipes before tasting a traditional Yorkshire Christmas pie and revealing the history of the Norfolk black turkey

programme.desc: Professional wrestling action, with some of the biggest names in comedy wrestling against each other and some of the UK's top wrestlers at the Hammersmith Apollo in London. The card features Ed Gamble wrestling against Phil Wang in the headline title match, James Acaster issuing an open challenge to fight any comedian or wrestler who can handle him, and Maisie Adam facing multi-time champion wrestler Nina Samuels. Other comedians taking part include Max Olesker, Ivan Gonzalez, Ania Magliano, Harriet Kemsley, Amy Gledhill, Sarah Keyworth, Abi Clarke, Max Fosh, Mark Silcox, Adam Kay, Rose Matafeo and Rosie Jones. There is commentary in the venue from Greg James, Sara Pascoe and Nish Kumar, backstage reporting from Olga Koch and Matthew Crosby, Ivo Graham as the event emcee, and Joe Lycett as the event's CEO.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Frank announces his engagement, but Lee cannot believe that any woman would want to marry his father so Lucy invites Frank and his beau Carol to dinner to allay Lee's misgivings. When Carol turns out to be perfect, Lee becomes even more nervous fearing that if she dumps his dad, it'll be his shoulder that he will cry on. In an effort to determine Carol's motivations, Lee joins Frank's dating app and puts himself forward as a potential date for her. If she bites, he will know she's up to no good. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Bobby Ball and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance by Susie Blake

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Suspicions are raised when a routine x-ray reveals suspicious packages hidden inside the tyres of a pick-up truck. Officers also seize a large shipment of marijuana

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Bologna in northern Italy, a city so famous for its food that it's known as the stomach of Italy. He learns how to make the local fresh egg pasta used for tortellini, tagliatelle, ravioli and lasagne and enjoys stuffed rabbit, filled with Parma ham and Parmesan frittata for dinner. Later, Rick drives up into the hills to a cheese dairy where he tastes a 13-year aged Parmesan and enjoys lunch in at a pig farm with restaurant attached, where huge charcoal-grilled pork steaks are served

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Palermo in northern Sicily, a city echoing Arab, North African, French, Spanish and Italian influences, making its cuisine unique and irresistible. Pine nuts, raisins, almonds and golden breadcrumbs are combined with flavours of sun-ripened tomatoes, lemons and the finest olive oil to create a range of unforgettable dishes. Rick witnesses locals celebrating a festival in honour of Palermo's patron saint Rosalia, and receives a cookery lesson from a real-life duchess. At home he cooks Marsala chicken, a Sicilian classic served in UK bistros since the 1960s

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond tests the Smart car, which is fitted with dual engines and turbos, James May considers the rejuvenation of Birmingham and Jaguar's XJ6, and Jeremy Clarkson compares two Type R Honda cars with the Civic and NSX. Carol Vorderman is this week's Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and, with the Olympic Games just around the corner, the team invent a long-jump competition for vehicles

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine, featuring a race to Monte Carlo between Jeremy Clarkson in an Aston Martin DB9, and Richard Hammond and James May, who opt to take a high-speed train. Actress Fay Ripley is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, while a nun swaps a Vauxhall Nova for a monster truck

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: The pair fish at Christchurch's Bridge Pool in Dorset, hoping to catch sea trout, before Paul persuades Bob to charter a boat in search of sea bass. They venture out to the Needles where Paul makes a incredible catch, and decide to take the fish to a local restaurant to prepare it

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon explores the eastern Mediterranean, beginning in Cyprus, where he goes on patrol with UN peacekeepers overseeing an abandoned buffer zone. He moves on to Lebanon, where he discovers the roots of Christianity, and Israel, where he boards a navy vessel guarding the country's offshore oil reserves. Finally, he crosses the world's most heavily fortified borders into Gaza, where he meets a woman helping reconstruction efforts by making bricks into ash

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore more of the nation's dishes, beginning by showing there is more to British fowl than roast chicken. programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Charlotte buys an 18th-century portrait at auction, but on returning home is attacked by a robber, who steals the prestigious item. Looking out the window, she sees Lovejoy's pick-up speeding away. When quizzed, the dealer finds the events of that night difficult to recall as he had been seriously inebriated. Ian McShane and Caroline Langrishe star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A deranged murderer with a vendetta against Lovejoy kidnaps and threatens to kill Charlotte, trying to lure the antiques dealer into a trap via a series of fiendish puzzles. Christopher Fairbank (Moxey in Auf Wiedersehen, Pet) guest stars, with Ian McShane and Caroline Langrishe

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Concern grows after a seemingly depressed Tinker goes missing without explanation. Lovejoy eventually tracks him down to his sister's hotel on Burgh Island, which is about to be lost to a property developer. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest investigates the mysterious death of a beekeeper. Nigel Planer guest stars in the 1950s-set murder mystery series

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is suspended after assaulting a high-ranking officer in the Met, suspecting him of covering up a death in custody years before. Meanwhile, an Argentinian pistol recovered from the Thames is linked to two unsolved murders which took place 16 years apart - and with one of them having occurred in Gibraltar, Sandra takes the team - minus Brian - on an Iberian adventure to uncover the truth. Alun Armstrong, Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star in the crime drama

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: As the Gibraltar locals reel from the murder of shipping agent Gordon Fletcher, Brian and Gerry - last seen being carried off in a shipping container - find themselves stranded on the side of a Spanish mountain, and now face the problem of how to get back to the rest of the team. As Sandra attempts to unravel secrets stretching back over three decades and link what has now become three murders, she still has doubts about the innocence of charming entrepreneur Harry Truman - but that doesn't stop her joining him for lunch on his luxury yacht. Conclusion of a two-part story, with regulars Amanda Redman, Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson joined by Vincent Regan and George Irving

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unusual advertisement appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local gazette, purporting to reveal details of a forthcoming murder, including the date, time and venue. It appears to be a morbid practical joke, but proves shockingly accurate. Puzzled by the mystery, and with more suspects than he can shake a stick at, Inspector Craddock quickly enlists Miss Marple's help in solving the crime. Joan Hickson stars

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: When Brokenwood's elite begin to act strangely, the occurrences come to a head after a wealthy man is crushed by a chandelier in his home; as Shepherd investigates, he uncovers a tangled mystery filled with greed and revenge

programme.title: Monsieur Spade
programme.desc: Spade gets more than he bargained for when he questions his eccentric neighbours and learns more about the boy's mysterious abilities. Meanwhile, Jean-Pierre faces his past

programme.title: Monsieur Spade
programme.desc: Spade discovers who has been following him, while Philippe sets up a deal for the boy that quickly goes sideways - leading to a dangerous confrontation

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is suspended after assaulting a high-ranking officer in the Met, suspecting him of covering up a death in custody years before. Meanwhile, an Argentinian pistol recovered from the Thames is linked to two unsolved murders which took place 16 years apart - and with one of them having occurred in Gibraltar, Sandra takes the team - minus Brian - on an Iberian adventure to uncover the truth. Alun Armstrong, Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star in the crime drama

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A woman is caught stealing milk from Nonnatus House, but she pleads for mercy from sisters Julienne and Monica Joan, explaining that she has been stealing milk as she is pregnant. As Sister Julienne learns more about her, she is convinced that this woman needs help becoming the mother she wants to be. Meanwhile, Dr Turner puzzles over a mysterious string of symptoms, and the blossoming romance between Miss Higgins and Sgt Woolf is thrown into jeopardy. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Great Slaughter plays host to the summer roadshow of a popular children's TV programme Jolly Roger. Sister Boniface is called in to investigate when the editor is found murdered on set during the live show. Lorna Watson stars as the nun, moped rider, wine maker and part-time forensic scientist

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: After a man is fatally stabbed during a séance, and no weapon is found, Shepherd digs into the eerie incident to catch the murderer; questioning if supernatural forces were involved, the team must separate superstition from reality

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Grandad has a fall and Adrian takes up art in a big way. Aveline has her first quarrel with her fiance and Shifty has a date

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Mrs Boswell bonds with new neighbour Celia, and Billy manages to upset Adrian in the middle of a poetry recital in the kitchen

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 26 October 1989 and featuring performances by Chris Rea, Adeva and Living in a Box

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Andy Crane present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 2 November 1989. This edition features performances by Deborah Harry, Phil Collins, Martika and Kylie Minogue. Plus, music by Mixmaster, The Beloved, Jive Bunny & The Mastermixers, Transvision Vamp, Milli Vanilli, D Mob Introducing Cathy Dennis and Luther Vandross

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Mayo presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on16 November 1989 and featuring performances by 808 State, Lisa Stansfield, Inner City, UB40, Kaoma, Iron Maiden and Phil Collins

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first shown on April 27, featuring performances by London Boys, Natalie Cole, De la Soul, Fine Young Cannibals, Morrissey, the Beatmasters with Merlin, Yazz, the Bangles and Poison

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first shown on May 25, featuring performances by Lynne Hamilton, Donna Summer and Edelweiss

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Gary Davies present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 1 June 1989. Featuring Sinitta, Fuzzbox and Neneh Cherry

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on June 8, featuring performances by the Beautiful South, Guns N' Roses and Jason Donovan

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first shown on June 29, featuring performances by Holly Johnson, Queen, Donna Allen, Guns N' Roses, London Boys and Double Trouble with Rebel MC

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Former Emmerdale actress Charlie Hardwick shows Robson the hidden gems of the East Durham coastline. The pair take an art class on the sand, a wellness choir teacher shows how singing outdoors can be good for mind and body, and Robson signs up for a session with a forest bathing instructor among the trees of Hamsterley Forest

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon continues to trace the path of the northern tropic around the globe, joining travellers on the coast of Western Sahara to partake in the extreme sport of kite surfing, and meeting activists who campaign for indigenous people's rights. He continues on through the world's largest minefield, takes a gruelling train ride across Mauritania and visits huge refugee camps in the Algerian desert

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of Simon's journey takes him from the banks of the Nile to the edge of the Indian Ocean in Oman. Along the way he is introduced to Nubians displaced by the formation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s and nomadic Bedouins who have been forced to move by a seven-year drought scientists believe is due to climate change. On Egypt's southern coast, he dives in pristine coral reefs, before seeing the unexpected sides of Saudi Arabian life

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Stage four of Simon's journey takes him across the areas of India rarely visited by tourists, including the unique desert environment of 'Little Rahn', home to the threatened wild ass, and Gandhi's home city of Ahmedabad, where he sees evidence of ethnic tensions between Hindus and Muslims. In Bhopal, he finds a city still contaminated by chemicals 25 years after an infamous industrial accident, while in the city of Kolkata he ponders the congestion that continues to spiral out of control

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon witnesses the effect of extreme poverty on the people of Bangladesh, where children are put to work for as little as 30 pence a day. His journey down the River Padma shows the threat of climate change on the densely populated country as melting Himalayan ice prompts the collapse of its banks, and introduces him to the national sport of Kabbadi, from which he escapes with only minor injuries. He then crosses the border to Burma to begin the most dangerous leg of his trip

programme.desc: The actor is joined by crime fiction author LJ Ross on a trip along the Northumbrian Coast. programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation

programme.desc: Vintage comedy sketches including Send in the Clones, Lightweight Louis Danvers and The Admirable Brighton. With guests Koo Stark and Barbara Dickson, who provides the music

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Barbara Dickson and Debbie Arnold guest star alongside Messrs Barker and Corbett, performing in a story called The Village of the Smiths

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including a Party sketch and An Appeal on Behalf of the Indecisive

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, with music by Elaine Paige singing Slap That Bass

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, with the duo having fun in spoof adventure Tinker Tailor Smiley Doyle. Plus, music by Elaine Paige

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including a Party sketch and An Appeal on Behalf of the Indecisive

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: The shopkeeping comedy returns with a festive instalment. Granville and Gastric find themselves playing marriage counsellors, while Leroy adopts a festive disguise to avoid relationship trouble of a different kind. Meanwhile, Mrs Featherstone struggles to get into the Christmas spirit and Kath attempts to bring the street together as one harmonious choir of carollers to beat Finkle Street once and for all - but does Granville have a trick up his sleeve to help them out? Starring David Jason, Tim Healy, James Baxter and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke. Featuring characters including Kevin and Perry, Tim Nice-But-Dim and the Self Righteous Brothers

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee is unhappy with his new living arrangements after Lucy's father separates from his wife and moves into the flat, so he takes on the role of counsellor in a bid to patch up the marriage and get his room back. Comedy, guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead, with Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy works on a government health initiative encouraging men to regularly check themselves for lumps, so an eager Lee follows the campaign's advice - only to discover something unexpected. Comedy, starring Sally Bretton, Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Katy Wix

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A shocking revelation throws the Ghosts into disarray. Kitty looks to Pat for moral support, and while he is more than happy to oblige, he finds himself in deep water trying to offer her words of comfort. Another bombshell drops when Mike realises there have been some crossed wires over an event booking and he and Alison are forced into hosting an impromptu - and ill-prepared - children's birthday bash in order not to let two little girls down. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie senses an opportunity to get himself knighted when Marlene becomes heavily involved in charity work. Enlisting the help of their staff, Marlene tries to make it happen using her contacts, but achieving her husband's dream turns out to be harder than expected. Comedy, starring John Challis

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin organises a fundraising dance, and suspects that Paul will sabotage his arrangements. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Peter Egan and Penelope Wilton. First broadcast February 26, 1984

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The disappearance of an illicit tin of pineapple chunks leaves the inmates horrified that there is a thief in their midst - and for a while, no one is above suspicion. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Animal rights activists target the village, with Llewellyn falling victim to a paint bomb and the pub coming under threat, so Boycie organises patrol groups to protect Winderdown farm from attack. Meanwhile, Tyler wants to borrow money to buy equipment for his band, but Boycie refuses until his son completes a task to prove his business acumen

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Humphries is accused of dipping his hand in the till. His distinguished career appears to end in disgrace when he is dismissed after being found guilty. Comedy, with John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo accidently discards something of sentimental value, and waxes lyrical about the two subjects closest to his heart - love and ferrets. Meanwhile, Seymour demonstrates his new home security device. Long-running rural comedy, starring Bill Owen and Michael Aldridge

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour reveals his latest invention - a purse for men. Compo agrees to test the product, which results in minor havoc. Comedy, with Bill Owen, Michael Aldridge and Peter Sallis

Eric, Cyril and Mr Newbold consider exploring their feminine side, while Mrs Featherstone is concerned that Mrs Rossi is becoming a bad influence on the shop, and Kath is on the receiving end of a new technique for matching customers to the perfect product

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville has to deal with a malfunctioning till, while Eric and Cyril decide it is time to show some macho spirit in the home of the Tour de Yorkshire and get back in the saddle. Meanwhile, Leroy tells Beth about his sneaky plan to find a blind date for Ruby, and Mr Newbold is sent on a mission by Mrs Featherstone to find out more about Mrs Rossi and whether she poses a threat to the local shop. But does he discover more than he bargained for?

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard finally move into their new country retreat, and while she thinks it is 'olde worlde bijou', he finds it a decided disappointment. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: The neighbourhood prepares for a wedding, and Granville gets into the spirit of things by putting up a large display to bring a bit of cheeriness to his shop window. However, the reluctant groom has cold feet, and it falls to the miserly shopkeeper to ensure he makes it to the church on time. The marital atmosphere also has a marked effect on Mrs Featherstone, who steps up her search for a fourth husband - with Granville and Mr Newbold finding themselves likely candidates to be the next lucky man

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: The shopkeeping comedy returns with a festive instalment. Granville and Gastric find themselves playing marriage counsellors, while Leroy adopts a festive disguise to avoid relationship trouble of a different kind. Starring David Jason, Tim Healy, James Baxter and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Tim puts the flat on the market in the wake of Kate's departure, forcing Lee to come up with a plan to allow him to stay put. However, moving the landlord's sister Lucy in proves far from ideal when the pair end up in a bidding war. Comedy, starring Lee Mack

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Newlyweds Dermot and Maria argue over whether they should buy their own home. Meanwhile, Grandad hopes to witness his own funeral, but Mrs Brown's plans go awry when she mistakes two Mormons - who have been stealing Father Quinn's parishioners - for the men who are supposed to be delivering the coffin

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike receive some exciting news about their guesthouse business, but the fallout soon sees tensions running high between the pair. Pat and Thomas learn about the challenges of veganism, Kitty attempts to adopt a new way of communicating that sparks confusion between her and Alison. Lady Button is delighted by a new arrival that allows her to tap into a past passion. Robin however is less charmed by the new addition. Julian is confronted by a hard truth, but is determined to prove everyone wrong. Alison and Mike reach boiling point as Robin comes face to face with his biggest fear

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of how the British sitcom was made, featuring unseen footage of Victoria Wood, and interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotter brothers are guests at a wedding, where they are intrigued to hear their dear departed mother inherited the ill-gotten gains of an East End bank robber - but nobody has ever found the loot. Christmas special of the long-running comedy, originally shown in 1987, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield, with a guest appearance by Joan Sims

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del buys a new manual to help him show Raquel more appreciation and give him the confidence to make positive decisions. Unfortunately, as a result of such upbeat thinking, the Trotters' cash flow hits an all-time low and an angry punter is left baying for blood. The second part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason, Tessa Peake-Jones and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Raquel invites her estranged parents around for dinner, and Del-Boy is keen to make an impression - but a mix-up with the gravy boat puts a damper on proceedings. However, things soon take a turn for the better when Del's 'father-in-law' finds an old watch among the Trotters' junk, which he claims could net them a pretty packet at auction. But nothing can quite prepare them for the shock they have in store. The final part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Miami Twice - Part One: The American Dream
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Trotters get a chance to escape Peckham for a holiday in the Florida sun - but it means Del leaving his newborn son behind. David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst star, with Buster Merryfield, Tessa Peake-Jones, Gwyneth Strong, Denis Lill and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Miami Twice - Part Two: Oh to Be in England
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Trotters arrive in Florida, and walk straight into disaster after their camper van is burgled and local Mafiosi take a shine to Del-Boy, who bears more than a passing resemblance to their boss. What Del does not know is that they are planning to have him killed as a way of faking the godfather's death to help him escape being sent to prison. Feature-length episode of the comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst, with a cameo appearance by Bee Gee Barry Gibb

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the sci-fi comedy series. After three million years in suspended animation, Dave Lister awakes to find he's the last surviving member of the human race, with only a mutated cat and a hologram of his least favourite crewmate for company. Craig Charles and Chris Barrie star

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The ship exceeds the speed of light, allowing the crew to see echoes of future events, but while Rimmer gets a pleasant surprise, Lister is shocked to see what appears to be his own violent death. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles and Danny John-Jules

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin and Perry try to befriend some girls at a party, while Tim Nice-But-Dim transforms himself into a rock star, and Lance sinks into depression when his imaginary girlfriend dumps him. Comedy sketches, featuring Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke
